postfx.summary: Grade-chain review of PostFX.tsx + constants.ts (verified against shipped postprocessing/@react-three/postprocessing sources). The core architecture is sound and well co-designed: the bloom knee smoothstep(1.0, 1.9) maps precisely onto the scene's luminance ladder (rim 1.2-1.6 -> 13-74% mask, filaments 2.2 -> full), GradePre's log pivot 0.4135884 is correct mid-grey, the shadow tint is exactly luma-neutral (0.5009), and CA/vignette/noise values are restrained. Top issues: (1) MUST — the bloom tuning contract is stale in 5+ comment sites (0.55/0.65 vs live 1.0/2.5), corrupting the declared single-source-of-truth tuning interface; (2) highTint luma 0.5299 quietly re-lifts post-AgX highlights ~3%, working against the protected rose gradation — fix to [0.52, 0.50, 0.44] (luma 0.500, same chroma spread); (3) vibrance lacks a highlight guard and can push values past 1.0 post-tonemap, re-introducing micro-clipping — add a (1 - smoothstep(0.75, 1.0, l)) fade; (4) MSAA double-pay: canvas antialias buffer is allocated but unused while the composer runs multisampling 0 — gate 4x MSAA to high tier and drop canvas antialias. Remaining items are doc-coupling notes (pre-exposure threshold math, inverted convolution claim, 1.6-vs-1.45 drift) and a tier-consistency option for mipmap bloom. All fixes are sub-perceptual micro-refinements; nothing removes motion or alters the canon look.

items.name: Stale bloom contract: comments cite threshold 0.55 / intensity 0.65 against live values 1.0 / 2.5
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:156-157 says 'Bloom: 0.55 threshold catches stars/aura glow; intensity raised to 0.65' but constants.ts:193 is `bloom: { intensity: 2.5, luminanceThreshold: 1.0, luminanceSmoothing: 0.9 }` and constants.ts:192 states the opposite intent ('ONLY the ultra-bright neon mesh and lobes glow' — NeuralAura.tsx:27 holds shells under 0.5, KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:219 keeps 80% of sky < 0.04, so neither blooms). CognitiveGrasp.tsx:292, 446, 450, 505-506 still encode the ladder contract as 'peak emission stays under the 0.55 bloom threshold'. The real knee is smoothstep(1.0, 1.9, L) (postprocessing build/index.js:3460), co-designed with the ladder at SuperbrainScene.tsx:516-519 (rim 1.2-1.6 -> 13%-74% mask, filaments 2.2 -> 100%). Anyone tuning from PostFX's comment will misplace the knee against the ladder.
items.fix: Comment-only, zero visual change. Replace PostFX.tsx:156-157 with: 'Bloom: threshold 1.0 + smoothing 0.9 -> knee = smoothstep(1.0, 1.9, sceneLuma), sampled PRE-exposure. Ladder: rim 1.2-1.6 blooms 13-74%, filaments ~2.2 bloom fully; everything clamped <=0.5 stays dark. intensity 2.5.' Update the four CognitiveGrasp.tsx comments (292, 446, 450, 505-506) from 'under the 0.55 bloom threshold' to 'under the 1.0 bloom threshold (knee starts at 1.0; clamp 0.5 leaves 2x headroom)'.

items.name: highTint is not luma-neutral: soft-light adds ~+3% highlight luma after AgX, nudging the crown back toward the plateau
items.evidence: constants.ts:204 `highTint: [0.55, 0.53, 0.47]` has Rec.709 luma 0.5299; soft-light with s>0.5 lifts (PostFX.tsx:84-91), so highlights gain ~3% luma post-tonemap — exactly counter to constants.ts:188-189 ('rose GRADATION, not a flat clipped plateau') and constants.ts:205 ('keeps the bright rose brain clean'). The shadowTint [0.42, 0.52, 0.55] (constants.ts:203) IS luma-neutral at 0.5009 — the split-tone is asymmetric by accident, not design.
items.fix: constants.ts:204: change highTint from [0.55, 0.53, 0.47] to [0.52, 0.50, 0.44] (Rec.709 luma 0.4999; identical R-B chroma spread of 0.08, same amber hue). The split-tone becomes pure chroma like the shadow side; sub-perceptual everywhere except it stops re-brightening the crown's path to white.

items.name: Vibrance can push post-tonemap values above 1.0, re-introducing the hard clip AgX was added to remove
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:107-109: `c = l + (c - l) * (1.0 + uVibrance * (1.0 - clamp(sat, 0.0, 1.0)));` runs in linear AFTER AgX with no luma guard and no clamp. With vibrance 0.21 (constants.ts:206), a bright mid-saturation pixel e.g. (1.0, 0.55, 0.6): l=0.649, sat=0.45, scale=1.116 -> R=1.04, which hard-clips at the final sRGB encode (and Vignette NORMAL + Noise ADD operate on the out-of-range value first). It also partially undoes AgX's signature desaturate-toward-white on the brightest pixels; the (1-sat) guard protects high-chroma filaments but not high-luma rose.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:109: add a highlight fade inside the vibrance term: `c = l + (c - l) * (1.0 + uVibrance * (1.0 - clamp(sat, 0.0, 1.0)) * (1.0 - smoothstep(0.75, 1.0, l)));`. Vibrance 0.21 is untouched for the nebula/mids (l < 0.75) and ramps to 0 at display white — no overshoot, AgX's approach-to-white preserved.

items.name: MSAA double-pay: canvas antialias buffer is paid for but unused, while the composer runs with multisampling 0
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:155 `<EffectComposer multisampling={0}>` (library default is 8, @react-three/postprocessing src/EffectComposer.tsx:68) while WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:213 sets `antialias: true`. With the composer mounted, geometry renders into the composer's non-MSAA HalfFloat input buffer; the canvas's MSAA framebuffer only ever receives the final fullscreen quad (no geometric edges), so its memory/resolve cost buys nothing. With dpr capped at 1.5 on high tier (WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:21-24), brain-silhouette and panel-frame edges alias.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:155: `multisampling={tier === 'high' ? 4 : 0}` (4x MSAA on the composer input buffer, high tier only — a lift, never a degrade). WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:214 area: set `antialias: false` to stop allocating the dead canvas MSAA buffer (visually identical; the final pass is a textured quad).

items.name: Bloom threshold is pre-exposure but the exposure tuning range is documented without the coupling
items.evidence: Bloom is the first pass (PostFX.tsx:158-165), so its luminance pass reads raw scene values; exposure 1.45 is applied later inside AgX (build/index.js:13481, three's AgXToneMapping multiplies by toneMappingExposure). constants.ts:188 invites tuning exposure across 1.4-1.9, but moving exposure shifts what 'reads as white' without moving the bloom cut — at exposure 1.9, a scene value of 0.76 already renders white-hot yet gets zero bloom (threshold 1.0, constants.ts:193).
items.fix: Add one comment line at constants.ts:192: 'luminanceThreshold is PRE-exposure scene luminance. If toneMappingExposure moves off 1.45, scale threshold by (1.45 / newExposure) to keep bloom locked to perceived brightness (e.g. exposure 1.9 -> threshold 0.76, knee width 0.9 -> 0.69).'

items.name: PostFX header doc drift: exposure '1.6' and inverted convolution/pass-split claim
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:15 says 'WorkspaceCanvas sets it to 1.6' — actual value is POST_FX.toneMappingExposure = 1.45 (constants.ts:190, wired at WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:221). PostFX.tsx:23-24 says 'only convolution effects like Bloom split passes' — verified inverted: ChromaticAberrationEffect carries EffectAttribute.CONVOLUTION (postprocessing build/index.js:4594), BloomEffect does not; per the grouping loop (@react-three/postprocessing src/EffectComposer.tsx:146-153) the high tier builds 3 EffectPasses [Bloom], [CA], [GradePre+AgX+GradePost+Vignette+Noise], and medium tier merges everything into 1.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:15: replace '1.6' with 'POST_FX.toneMappingExposure (currently 1.45)'. PostFX.tsx:23-24: replace with 'CA is the only CONVOLUTION effect here — at high tier it splits the chain into 3 EffectPasses ([Bloom], [CA], [grades+AgX+Vignette+Noise]); Bloom itself merges. Expected grade cost < 0.5 ms.'

items.name: Bloom character shifts between tiers: medium tier silently swaps mipmap blur for Kawase blur at the same intensity 2.5
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:163 `mipmapBlur={tier === 'high'}` — at medium tier the same intensity 2.5 / threshold 1.0 (constants.ts:193) drives postprocessing's legacy Kawase-blur bloom, whose falloff is tighter and energy response differs from the wide mipmap chain the values were graded against, so filament halos read harsher on medium without any constant changing.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:163: change to `mipmapBlur` (always true whenever Bloom renders — i.e. `mipmapBlur={true}`); the mipmap downsample chain is comparable in cost to multi-pass Kawase and keeps one bloom look across tiers. If medium-tier GPU headroom is a concern instead, keep the gate but add a tier value in constants.ts: `bloomIntensityKawase: 1.8` and wire `intensity={tier === 'high' ? POST_FX.bloom.intensity : POST_FX.bloom.bloomIntensityKawase}`.

postfx.summary: Reviewed PostFX.tsx + constants.ts against the installed postprocessing 6.39.1 sources. Verified clean: chain order (Bloom→CA→GradePre→AgX→GradePost→Vignette→Noise) is correct; no exposure double-pay (the canvas ACESFilmic prop is inert, AgX consumes toneMappingExposure=1.45 exactly once); CA (0.00055, radial-modulated), vignette (0.28/0.62), and premultiplied grain (0.025) are all properly restrained — no changes proposed there. Five findings, all micro-refinements of what exists: (1) MUST — multisampling={0} contradicts the canvas's own '4x MSAA at high tier' design note, leaving the frame with zero AA at dpr<=1.5; one-line fix restores the documented canon. (2) SHOULD — Bloom's implicit SCREEN blend (`dst+src-min(dst*src,1)`) subtracts energy from HDR cores (2.2-luminance filaments dim by up to 1-src) while being numerically identical to ADD over the dark void; switching to ADD at the same 2.5 intensity is a core-only, AgX-rolled lift. (3) SHOULD — mipmapBlur={tier==='high'} swaps medium tier onto the deprecated Kawase path, changing the halo shape under the same 2.5 intensity; always-true keeps the canon footprint. (4) SHOULD — stale comments (0.55 threshold / 0.65 intensity / 1.6 exposure, plus CognitiveGrasp's 0.55-budget clamps) misdocument the real smoothstep(1.0,1.9) raw-HDR ladder and will misdirect the next tune; comment-only fix. (5) COULD — vibrance 0.21 can overshoot 1.0 post-AgX causing per-channel clip hue-skew on the crown; one-line hue-preserving normalize.

items.name: Composer MSAA is 0 despite the canvas documenting 4x MSAA at high tier — no AA exists anywhere
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:155 `<EffectComposer multisampling={0}>` while WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:213-216 disables canvas AA (`antialias: false`) with the comment 'The composer owns AA (4x MSAA on its input buffer at high tier — PostFX.tsx)'. The composer does NOT own it — nothing does. With TIER_DPR capped at [1, 1.5] (WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:21-24), high-tier panel borders and the brain silhouette render with zero antialiasing; edge crawl shows in motion (parallax, panel drag).
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:155 → `<EffectComposer multisampling={tier === 'high' ? 4 : 0}>` (tier is already in scope from line 147). This implements the exact behavior WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:213-215 already documents as the canon design — restoration, not redesign. Tier only changes on operator click (QualityTierProvider.tsx:133-139), so the composer rebuild cost is a non-issue.

items.name: Bloom's default SCREEN blend erodes the HDR cores it is supposed to glorify
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:159-164 passes no blendFunction, so postprocessing 6.39.1 BloomEffect defaults to BlendFunction.SCREEN: `c = dst + src - min(dst*src, 1.0)` (node_modules/postprocessing/build/index.js, screen.frag). On the luminance ladder's hot pixels (SuperbrainScene.tsx:516-519: filaments ~2.2, rim 1.2-1.6) wherever the blurred bloom texel src < 1 but dst*src >= 1, the blend DARKENS the core by (1-src) — e.g. dst=2.2, src=0.6 → 1.8, below the unbloomed 2.2. The `min()` also puts a slope kink at the halo→core transition. Over the near-black void (dst≈0.04) screen and add are numerically identical (difference = dst*src ≈ 0.04·src), so the visible halo field is untouched by the fix.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx Bloom: add `blendFunction={BlendFunction.ADD}` (BlendFunction already imported, line 38), keep intensity 2.5 / threshold 1.0 / smoothing 0.9 unchanged. Only pixels where dst*src is non-negligible change — exactly the cores screen was subtracting from; AgX's shoulder rolls the restored energy into rose gradation (its documented job, PostFX.tsx:180-181). Verify the crown gradation in an A/B before landing.

items.name: Medium tier silently swaps bloom algorithms — same intensity 2.5 on a different halo footprint
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:163 `mipmapBlur={tier === 'high'}`. In postprocessing 6.39.1, mipmapBlur=false falls back to the DEPRECATED KawaseBlurPass path (resolutionScale 0.5, KernelSize.LARGE — BloomEffect ctor defaults), producing a tighter, differently-shaped halo. intensity 2.5 / threshold 1.0 / smoothing 0.9 (constants.ts:193) were tuned against the mipmap footprint, so an operator who picks 'medium' gets a different canon look (hotter, tighter glow), not merely cheaper pixels — violating 'medium = same look, less resolution'.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:163 → `mipmapBlur` (always true). The 8-level mip chain costs about the same as the LARGE Kawase kernel at half resolution, and bloom is already fully disabled at 'low' (PostFX.tsx:158), so the perf ladder is preserved while the bloom shape stays canon on every tier that has bloom.

items.name: Stale luminance-ladder numbers in comments (0.55 threshold, 0.65 intensity, 1.6 exposure) will poison the next tune
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:156-157 says 'Bloom: 0.55 threshold catches stars/aura glow; intensity raised to 0.65' but constants.ts:193 ships `{ intensity: 2.5, luminanceThreshold: 1.0, luminanceSmoothing: 0.9 }`. PostFX.tsx:15 says 'WorkspaceCanvas sets it to 1.6' but constants.ts:190 ships 1.45. Downstream, CognitiveGrasp.tsx:288-292, 446-450, 505-506 clamp packet/flare luminance to <= 0.5 'under the 0.55 bloom threshold' — the real budget is smoothstep(1.0, 1.9, l) on RAW pre-exposure values (confirmed in postprocessing build: `mask=smoothstep(threshold,threshold+smoothing,l)`), so those elements have ~2x more headroom than their comments claim. Anyone tuning an aura to 0.8 'to catch bloom' per the 0.55 comment gets silently nothing.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx:156-157 → 'Bloom: threshold 1.0 on RAW pre-exposure HDR (knee 1.0→1.9 via smoothing 0.9): rim 1.2-1.6 blooms partially (12-74%), filaments ~2.2 fully; intensity 2.5 because AgX compresses the result'. PostFX.tsx:15 → '1.45'. CognitiveGrasp.tsx:292/450/506 → replace '0.55 bloom threshold' with '1.0 bloom threshold (knee starts at 1.0)'. Comment-only; zero rendered pixels change.

items.name: GradePost vibrance can overshoot 1.0 post-AgX — final sRGB encode clips per-channel and skews crown hue
items.evidence: PostFX.tsx:107-109 with vibrance 0.21 (constants.ts:206): `c = l + (c - l) * (1.0 + uVibrance * (1.0 - sat))` runs on display-referred [0,1] AgX output and can push a channel past 1.0 (e.g. rose crown (1.0, 0.5, 0.6): l=0.6135, sat=0.5, factor 1.105 → r=1.041). Vignette/Noise pass it through and the composer's final sRGB encode clamps per channel, rotating hue slightly on exactly the hottest, most-looked-at pixels.
items.fix: PostFX.tsx: after line 109 add `c /= max(1.0, max(c.r, max(c.g, c.b)));` — hue-preserving normalization, a no-op for every pixel already <= 1.0.

interaction.summary: Interaction-state review of SuperbrainHUD.tsx, ApprovalPanel.tsx, RegionPins.tsx and globals.css. Two musts: the region pin chip (role=button, tabIndex=0) has no Enter/Space handler so it is mouse-only, and the entire stylesheet has zero :focus-visible rules — keyboard users get only the UA default ring on every control (sovereignty row, mode rail, ghost-plus, shield, approval buttons, Execute). Shoulds: the pin chip has cursor:pointer but no :hover and no aria-expanded; the FIDELITY/SKY/SURFACE/SOUND hover snaps (no transition on .fidelity-button strong); no control except Execute has any :active pressed feedback; SOUND lacks aria-pressed; the mode rail's active item is class-only; and the source-list aria-live region wraps rAF-tweened numbers that flood screen readers ~21 updates per 350ms tween. Coulds: ghost-plus buttons are no-ops wearing cursor:pointer and action-promising aria-labels, the CORE ONLINE/LINK OFFLINE flip is unannounced, and sovereignty-row hit targets are ~14px (padding: 0) vs the 24px minimum. Every fix is a micro-refinement using values already in the file's own design language (white-alpha brightening, 200ms ease-out-quart, the --open border color); nothing removes motion or alters the canon look.

interaction.summary: Interaction-state review of SuperbrainHUD.tsx, ApprovalPanel.tsx, RegionPins.tsx (pin chips) and the control CSS in globals.css. Three must-fix items: pin chips with role=button are mouse-only (no Enter/Space handler, no aria-expanded), the intake source-list is an aria-live region fed by a 60fps rAF tween (screen-reader flood), and the mode rail's active mode is exposed visually but not via aria-pressed. Systemic should-fix gaps: globals.css contains zero :focus-visible and zero :active rules (every control falls back to the UA ring; only Execute has press feedback via whileTap), and the pin chip has cursor:pointer with no hover state. Smaller refinements: aria-pressed on the SOUND toggle, aria-live on the shield's HOLD/TAMPER readout, inert ghost-plus/shield buttons carrying pointer affordances, and a stray brace at globals.css:1587. All fixes are additive micro-changes using existing canon values (accent #5ce1e6 hairlines, the --open border color, the established -1px/+1px translate grammar); no motion removed, no defaults touched.

cortex-shader.summary: Shader micro-review of SuperbrainScene.tsx (cortex GLSL, casing GLSL, region constants, shared uniforms). The canon look is intact overall, but one piece of documented canon wiring is severed: the cortex thought-wave term vanished in the v6 rewrite — THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL is imported but never injected, the baked objectPos attribute has zero consumers, and the wave scheduler feeds uWaveOrigins/uWaveTimes every frame to a shader that ignores them, so 'tool lights the lobe' currently only biases firefly blink (must: reconnect with gain 2.2 / sharpness 9.0 per the in-file ladder). Two coverage gaps on the casing layer: no dither on its faint additive fresnel ramp (banding on medium/low tiers where PostFX Noise grain is absent — fix with the project's own KnowledgeHorizon IGN idiom at ±1/255) and no uHold uniform (the brightest cyan rim ignores the amber approval hold — fix with the cortex's exact amber and 0.85 cap). Four micro lifts, all value-exact and visually inert or operator-gated: three divergent hold-amber constants (#ffce81-equivalent vs #b96a14 vs #ffb454) need a single source of truth; the wave GLSL pops 0.8% at its hard age-6 cutoff and duplicates BRAIN_SCALE 3.02 as a magic number; unbounded uTime quantizes shader animation after ~24 h (wrap at 1200π with wave-stamp continuity) and the sin-hash risks mediump GPUs (Hoskins swap scoped to the low tier only); the cortex rim pulse runs a private 0.32 Hz sine against the file's own 'one shared breath' doctrine, and the luminance-ladder comments lag the v6 gains (web 4.0 / rim 3.0).

items.name: Cortex thought-wave layer is severed (scheduler feeds uniforms nothing on the cortex consumes)
items.evidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:10 imports THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL but it is never interpolated into the onBeforeCompile cortex shader (emission block lines 753-789 has no thoughtWave() call and lines 644-647 link only uTime/uHold — never uWaveOrigins/uWaveTimes). The bake comment (lines 220-223, 307) promises an objectPos attribute 'so the shaders can evaluate filaments and thought-waves', and it is baked at line 452 — but no shader reads it (grep: zero consumers). The luminance-ladder doc (lines 516-519) and the wave-scheduler doc (lines 461-463: 'the SAME shard always lights the SAME cortex zone — causality is what upgrades animated to sentient') both promise cortex wavefronts; the frame loop still writes uWaveOrigins/uWaveTimes every frame (lines 1241-1246). Today only the CorticalSignals fireflies (CorticalSignals.tsx:91, sharpness 3.0 'wider than the cortex's tight front' — a comment that references the now-missing cortex front) show any wave. The label-anchored lobe lighting (THE LIVING TURN, lines 487-495, 1050-1063) currently only biases firefly blink, not the lobe surface — the v6 rewrite (cache key superbrain_v6, line 798) dropped the wiring, not the design.
items.fix: Reconnect, don't redesign: (1) at lines 646-647 add `shader.uniforms.uWaveOrigins = uniforms.uWaveOrigins; shader.uniforms.uWaveTimes = uniforms.uWaveTimes;` (2) in the vertex <common> patch (line 652) add `attribute vec3 objectPos; varying vec3 vObjectPos;` and in <begin_vertex> (line 658) add `vObjectPos = objectPos;` (3) in the fragment <common> patch (line 665) add `varying vec3 vObjectPos;` plus `${THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL}` (the import already exists); (4) after the rim term (line 779) add `totalEmissiveRadiance += (safeColor * 0.6 + vec3(0.4)) * thoughtWave(vObjectPos, uTime, 9.0) * 2.2;` — gain 2.2 = the documented filament/wavefront tier of the ladder (line 519) and the PostFX bloom-knee co-design (PostFX.tsx:159-163); sharpness 9.0 = the tight hairline front the firefly comment contrasts its 3.0 against. objectPos is brain-group-local — the exact space uWaveOrigins are authored in (WAVE_REGION_ANCHORS, lines 468-475), so anchors land on the right lobes.

items.name: Casing fresnel glow has no dither — banding on the dark brain face, worst on medium/low tiers
items.evidence: CASING_FRAGMENT_SHADER (SuperbrainScene.tsx:580-604) outputs a smooth additive ramp: effective add = emission*alpha = (mix(glow,irid,0.5)*fres*3.0)*(fres*0.4) ≈ 1.2*fres^2 falling to 0 toward the brain center over a near-black base (diffuse 0.01/0.02/0.04, line 749). It renders on ALL tiers (line 893, unconditional). The only grain in the pipeline is PostFX's Noise, gated to tier==='high' (PostFX.tsx:202-208), and Bloom (which would mask banding) is absent on low (PostFX.tsx:164) — so medium/low quantize the faint cyan ramp into visible 8-bit bands after AgX + GradePost. The project already has a named cure: KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:79-82 'Interleaved gradient noise — final-color dither' and :244 'Mandatory dither — kills the banding'.
items.fix: Apply the house IGN idiom to the casing: in CASING_FRAGMENT_SHADER, between line 598 (emission) and line 602 (gl_FragColor) insert `float ign = fract(52.9829189 * fract(dot(gl_FragCoord.xy, vec2(0.06711056, 0.00583715)))); emission += (ign - 0.5) * (2.0 / 255.0);` — identical constants to KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:81, amplitude ±1/255 (sub-perceptual; pure quantization noise-shaping, zero look change).

items.name: Approval-hold amber skips the casing — the brightest cyan element ignores the hold
items.evidence: During uHold, the cortex tints amber at uHold*0.85 (SuperbrainScene.tsx:783-788), wires dim to 0.3 (NervousSystem.tsx:75), the aura lerps to amber (NeuralAura.tsx:345-347) — but the neuralSkin casing material receives only uTime (SuperbrainScene.tsx:815 `uniforms: { uTime: uniforms.uTime }`) and CASING_FRAGMENT_SHADER (580-604) has no uHold: its full-intensity cyan/iridescent rim (emission up to 3.0, blooming) keeps shimmering through the 'organism turns amber' moment the uniforms doc promises (lines 156-158).
items.fix: Line 815 → `uniforms: { uTime: uniforms.uTime, uHold: uniforms.uHold }`. In CASING_FRAGMENT_SHADER add `uniform float uHold;` (after line 581) and replace line 593 with `vec3 glowColor = mix(vec3(0.0, 0.8, 1.0), vec3(1.0, 0.62, 0.22), uHold * 0.85);` and line 598 with `vec3 emission = mix(glowColor, irid, 0.5 * (1.0 - uHold * 0.85)) * fres * 3.0;` — exactly the cortex's amber constant and 0.85 cap (lines 783-787), same intensity, same motion; zero change when uHold=0.

items.name: Three different hold-amber constants across layers — divergence trap
items.evidence: One semantic event, three ambers: cortex holdTone vec3(1.0, 0.62, 0.22) in linear (SuperbrainScene.tsx:783, ≈ sRGB #ffce81, hue 36.7°); HOLD_TINT '#b96a14' (SuperbrainScene.tsx:179, linear ≈ (0.485, 0.144, 0.006), hue 31.3°) feeding uModeTint at line 1188; AURA_HOLD_AMBER '#ffb454' (NeuralAura.tsx:61, linear ≈ (1.0, 0.456, 0.089), hue 33.7°). The net composite is coherent today, but nothing ties them — the next tune of any one silently splits the hold into mismatched ambers.
items.fix: Centralize without changing a single rendered value: in SuperbrainScene export `const HOLD_AMBER = { cortexGlsl: 'vec3(1.0, 0.62, 0.22)', tint: '#b96a14', aura: '#ffb454' } as const;` with a one-line comment per role (cortex emission multiplier / uModeTint accent / aura atmosphere), template-inject HOLD_AMBER.cortexGlsl at line 783 (and into the casing fix above), import HOLD_AMBER.aura in NeuralAura.tsx:61. Byte-identical output; future edits move as one.

items.name: Thought-wave hard cutoff at age 6.0 pops, and the GLSL hard-codes 3.02 duplicating BRAIN_SCALE
items.evidence: CorticalSignals.tsx:41 `float live = step(0.0, age) * (1.0 - step(6.0, age));` kills the wave instantly at age 6 while exp(-a*0.8) still holds exp(-4.8) ≈ 0.0082 of peak — a 0.8% single-frame luminance pop on every firefly bias (and on the cortex front once finding 1 lands, ×2.2 gain ≈ 0.018 emission step). Separately, CorticalSignals.tsx:43 hard-codes `* 3.02` which must equal BRAIN_SCALE = 3.02 (SuperbrainScene.tsx:70) — but the two constants are unlinked, and the rendered scale actually breathes to ~3.22 at full burst (SuperbrainScene.tsx:844-845: ×(1+0.006·breath)+0.05·activity+0.15·burst), so the front-speed calibration already drifts ~6% during bursts; a future BRAIN_SCALE retune would silently desync wave speed everywhere.
items.fix: Two exact edits: (1) CorticalSignals.tsx:41 → `float live = step(0.0, age) * (1.0 - smoothstep(5.0, 6.0, age));` — bit-identical for age < 5, fades the residual 0.0082 to zero over the last second instead of popping. (2) Move `export const BRAIN_SCALE = 3.02` into src/lib/constants.ts (where POST_FX already lives), import it in both files, and build the GLSL line as `float d = distance(p, uWaveOrigins[i]) * ${BRAIN_SCALE.toFixed(2)};` — one source of truth, zero visual change.

items.name: Precision: unbounded uTime degrades shader animation in multi-hour sessions; sin-hash with ~1e5 arguments breaks on mediump GPUs
items.evidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1161 uploads raw elapsedTime forever; as a float32 uniform at t = 24 h (86400 s) the ulp is 0.0078 s, so sin(uTime*2.0) (line 775 pulse), sin(uTime*1.2 + 6.2831*h) (line 691 Voronoi anim) and the casing's uTime*0.1 irid (line 596) quantize into ~0.016 rad steps — visible micro-stutter in an always-on cockpit. Separately, hash33 (lines 670-675) is the classic fract(sin(dot))*43758 hash fed cell coords from vLocalPos = position*2.0 (line 659) where raw GLB positions reach ~±50 (root scale 0.01, line 233): dot products hit ~1e5, which is deterministic on desktop highp but produces blocky repeated cells on mediump-fragment mobile GPUs.
items.fix: CPU-side wrap with phase continuity: in the frame loop replace line 1161 with `const tWrap = time % (1200 * Math.PI); uniforms.uTime.value = tWrap;`, write wave stamps with the same clock at line 1244 (`uniforms.uWaveTimes.value[waves.slot] = tWrap;`), and on the wrap frame (tWrap < previous tWrap, keep a ref) subtract 1200*Math.PI from all three uWaveTimes entries. Wrap = 1200π ≈ 3769.911 s keeps |uTime| < 2^12 (ulp 0.00024 s) and is phase-seamless for every fixed in-file frequency (k·600 ∈ ℤ for k = 0.1, 1.2, 2.0); only the random-speed firefly blinks re-phase once per ~63 min, invisible in a blinker. For the hash: swap hash33 to the sin-free Hoskins form `p = fract(p * vec3(0.1031, 0.1030, 0.0973)); p += dot(p, p.yxz + 33.33); return fract((p.xxy + p.yxx) * p.zyx);` ONLY inside the existing tier==='low' GLSL branch (line 689 template) so the canon high-tier web stays bit-identical while weak GPUs get a robust hash.

items.name: Cortex pulse runs on its own 0.32 Hz metronome, decoupled from the shared breath doctrine; luminance-ladder doc is stale vs v6 gains
items.evidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:137-141 declares all layers 'phase-locked... the organism moves as one body', and lines 1163-1164 say the breath is 'never a constant ~1 Hz pulse' — yet the cortex rim pulses with its own constant sine `float pulse = sin(uTime * 2.0) * 0.5 + 0.5;` (line 775), ignoring uBreath entirely (uRimGain/uSssScale/uCoreGain are animated at lines 1171-1173 but the v6 cortex shader reads none of them). Also the ladder doc (lines 516-519: 'core ~0.18, SSS ~0.3, rim 1.2-1.6, filaments ~2.2') and PostFX.tsx:159-163 ('rim 1.2-1.6 blooms 13-74%') no longer match v6 reality: web peaks at 4.0 (line 766) and rim at fresnel*2.0*1.5 = 3.0 (lines 771-779), both fully past the 1.9 bloom knee.
items.fix: Operator's call (it changes a visible rhythm, so propose, don't apply): link `shader.uniforms.uBreath = uniforms.uBreath;` at line 647, declare `uniform float uBreath;` in the fragment common block, and replace lines 775-776 with `float pulseMix = mix(mix(0.7, 1.5, uBreath), 1.25, uHold);` — same 0.7-1.5 range, but phase-locked to the one shared systole and genuinely frozen mid-inhale during a hold (the doctrine at lines 156-158). Independently and risk-free: update the stale comment at lines 516-519 and PostFX.tsx:159-163 to the v6 numbers (web ≤4.0, rim ≤3.0, both bloom fully) so the next tuner calibrates against reality.

glass.summary: Glass/material review of GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/app/globals.css. Two structural pseudo-element collisions are silently corrupting the canon look: the wire-glow rules re-declare ::before on .command-bar (1186) and on the .glass-surface-bearing consoles (547), so the later rules strip the edge-lit rim gradient while the rim's surviving 1px xor-mask shrinks each 60px glow to an invisible blurred ring — on the command bar this also kills the --rim-top focus brightening, and on the consoles the glow box lands at the top instead of the bottom. Fixes preserve both effects exactly by relocating the glow (command bar → unused ::after; consoles → a dedicated .console-glow child, since ::after holds the scanline). The approval panel diverges from the established glass recipe on four axes: bare blur(14px) with no saturate(140%)/brightness(1.08), no -webkit-backdrop-filter (zero blur in Safari), no inset 0 1px 0 specular, and no .glass-grain child; plus 10px radius against the adjacent 14px command bar. Minor drift: a raw `ease 0.15s` transition off the motion tokens, two hand-rolled hairline colors, a missing specular on .region-pin, and three --mono token bypasses. All fixes are micro-refinements with exact values; no motion removed, no redesign.

items.name: Command bar ::before is double-booked: wire-glow overwrites the edge-lit rim and the rim's mask cripples the glow
items.evidence: globals.css:165 puts the rim-light frame on `.command-bar::before` (gradient riding --rim-top, masked to a 1px ring via lines 178-181), but globals.css:1186-1198 re-declares `.command-bar::before` as the wire glow. Same specificity, same @layer (components, lines 91-1587), later rule wins per-property: the rim's 180deg white gradient (171-177) is replaced by the wire gradient (1193), so the rim is gone and the `:focus-within { --rim-top: 0.24 }` toggle at 1202-1204 is dead. Meanwhile the rim's surviving `padding: 1px` (169) and xor mask (178-181) still apply to the glow, shrinking the intended 60px blurred wash into a blur(30px) 1px ring — both effects are corrupted on the live command bar.
items.fix: Move the wire glow to the command bar's unused ::after: change the selector at globals.css:1186 from `.command-bar::before` to `.command-bar::after`, keeping every declaration (top: -5px; left: 20%; right: 20%; height: 60px; the 3-stop gradient; filter: blur(30px); z-index: -1; animation: wireGlowSurge 3s ease-in-out infinite reverse) byte-identical. The rim at line 165 then renders again and --rim-top focus brightening comes back.

items.name: Console ::before collision: left/right consoles carry .glass-surface, so the bottom wire-glow kills the rim there too
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1031 and :1080 render `<aside className="left-console glass-surface">` / `"right-console glass-surface"`, so globals.css:547-559 (`.left-console::before, .right-console::before` wire glow) and globals.css:164 (`.glass-surface::before` rim) target the SAME pseudo-element. The later wire rule wins per-property: rim gradient replaced; and because `inset: 0` (167) still supplies `top: 0`, the over-constrained box (top: 0 + height: 60px beats bottom: -5px) parks the glow at the TOP of each console — opposite of the stated intent at line 545 ("surging UP into the bottom") — then the rim's surviving mask (178-181) reduces it to a 1px ring behind the panel (z-index: -1, line 556). Both consoles currently show neither a correct rim nor a visible glow. ::after is not available — it holds the scanline (564-577).
items.fix: Give the glow its own layer: add `<i className="console-glow" aria-hidden />` as the first child of each aside (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1031, 1080) and change the selector at globals.css:547 from `.left-console::before, .right-console::before` to `.left-console .console-glow, .right-console .console-glow`, dropping only `content: ''` and keeping position: absolute; bottom: -5px; left: 10%; right: 10%; height: 60px; the 4-stop gradient; filter: blur(30px); z-index: -1; animation: wireGlowSurge 3s ease-in-out infinite. z-index: -1 still nests correctly inside the aside's backdrop-filter stacking context, and overflow: hidden (line 401) keeps the original clip.

items.name: Approval panel misses the established glass recipe: bare blur, no -webkit- fallback, no specular inset
items.evidence: globals.css:1648 gives `.approval-panel` only `backdrop-filter: blur(14px)` — the canon recipe at 156-157 (and command bar 1180-1181) is `blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08)` PLUS the `-webkit-backdrop-filter` twin. The approval panel omits saturate/brightness (its glass reads flat and milky-dark next to the resaturated command bar it docks above) and omits `-webkit-backdrop-filter` entirely, so Safari renders zero blur. It also lacks the shared light-from-above specular: its box-shadow at 1647 is only the amber glow, vs the recipe's `inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1)` top edge (153, 1177).
items.fix: At globals.css:1647-1648 set: `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1), 0 0 26px rgba(255, 180, 84, 0.14);` and `backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08);`. Amber border and warm tint stay untouched.

items.name: Grain application gap: approval panel is the only glass pane without .glass-grain
items.evidence: The recipe comment at globals.css:185-186 says grain sits INSIDE each pane to kill banding and sell the material; the command bar and both consoles carry it (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1012, 1072, 1141), but ApprovalPanel.tsx:72's `<section className="approval-panel">` has no `.glass-grain` child — its large dark 0.78-alpha surface (globals.css:1644) is exactly where gradient banding shows.

No CSS change needed: .glass-grain (globals.css:187-196) uses border-radius: inherit and the panel already has overflow: hidden (1649); the section needs nothing else since .approval-panel children are positioned by its flex column and .glass-grain is absolute inset 0.

items.name: Panel radius drift: 16px / 14px / 10px across the three glass tiers, with 10 vs 14 directly adjacent
items.evidence: globals.css:151 `.glass-surface` radius 16px; globals.css:1174 `.command-bar` 14px; globals.css:1646 `.approval-panel` 10px. The approval panel renders 10px above the 14px command bar (margin: 0 auto 10px, line 1640) — two glass corners 10px apart with mismatched curvature, the most visible radius seam on screen.

It is the only transition in the file off the token system.

items.name: Hairline color drift inside the approval panel

items.name: Region pin lacks the shared light-from-above specular
items.evidence: The canon states one light direction everywhere: `.mode-num` (globals.css:690) and `.agent-avatar` (globals.css:1040) both carry `inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08)`. `.region-pin` (globals.css:462-479) is the same class of raised opaque chip (background rgba(5, 10, 18, 0.62)) but has no inset top highlight, so it reads flatter than every other chip under the same light.

glass.summary: Glass/material physics review of globals.css (1741 lines, read in full). Two MUST findings: (1) a pseudo-element collision — the wire-glow rules at :547 and :1186 silently override the canon edge-lit rim (:164) on both consoles AND the command bar at equal specificity, leaving the rim dead, the :focus-within --rim-top toggle inert, and the glow itself strangled by the rim's surviving 1px xor-mask (and on consoles mis-anchored to the TOP via over-constrained positioning); fix is `::before`→`::after` on the bar plus a dedicated `.console-glow` overlay element mirroring the existing console-surge pattern. (2) The approval panel breaks the resaturated-glass recipe: blur-only backdrop with no saturate(140%)/brightness(1.08) and no -webkit-backdrop-filter (Safari renders zero blur). SHOULD findings: approval panel also misses the canon shadow stack (no specular top edge / inner shade / 24px-70px depth shadow), drifts to a button-tier 10px radius beside the 14px command bar, and is the only glass pane with no glass-grain child (and lacks position:relative to host one); hover hairlines freehand across 0.18/0.16/0.14 for the same role. COULD: raw `ease 0.15s` transition off the motion tokens, three `var(--font-jetbrains-mono)` literals instead of --mono, and a 0.06 off-token hairline on the diff well. All fixes are micro-refinements with exact values; no motion removed, no canon redesign.

items.name: Wire-glow ::before overrides the edge-lit rim on all three primary glass panes (rim dead, glow misplaced and mask-strangled, focus brightening dead)
items.evidence: globals.css:164-183 defines the canon edge-lit rim on `.glass-surface::before, .command-bar::before` (1px padded frame, xor mask, top stop riding --rim-top). globals.css:547-559 then redefines `.left-console::before, .right-console::before` as the wire glow, and globals.css:1186-1198 redefines `.command-bar::before` as the command-bar glow. Both asides ARE `.glass-surface` (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1031, 1080), and all rules sit in the same @layer components at equal specificity, so the later glow rules win: the rim gradient is fully overridden on every console and the command bar. Worse, the rim's surviving longhands corrupt the glow: `padding: 1px` + the xor mask (175-181) clip the 60px glow strip to a 1px ring before `filter: blur(30px)`, and on the consoles the rim's `inset: 0` leaves `top: 0`, which with `height: 60px` over-constrains positioning so `bottom: -5px` is ignored — the underglow renders at the TOP of each console instead of rising from the wires at the bottom. Finally `.command-bar:focus-within { --rim-top: 0.24 }` (globals.css:1202-1204) is a dead toggle because nothing consumes --rim-top on the bar anymore.
items.fix: Two micro-moves, zero visual redesign: (1) globals.css:1186 — change selector `.command-bar::before` to `.command-bar::after` (the bar's ::after is unused; only consoles use ::after for the scanline), declarations unchanged. (2) Move the console glow off the contested pseudo onto a dedicated overlay, exactly like the established `.console-surge` pattern: rename `.left-console::before, .right-console::before` (globals.css:547) to `.console-glow`, keep every declaration byte-identical (position:absolute; bottom:-5px; left:10%; right:10%; height:60px; same 4-stop gradient; filter:blur(30px); z-index:-1; animation: wireGlowSurge 3s ease-in-out infinite; pointer-events:none), and render `<i className="console-glow" aria-hidden />` inside each aside next to the existing glass-grain (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1072 and 1141). This restores the visionOS rim + :focus-within brightening AND the intended bottom-anchored multicolor surge simultaneously.

items.name: Approval panel breaks the resaturated-glass optics: blur-only backdrop, no -webkit prefix (Safari gets zero blur)
items.evidence: globals.css:1648 — `.approval-panel { backdrop-filter: blur(14px); }` with no `-webkit-backdrop-filter` line at all. The canon recipe is `blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08)` with the -webkit twin on both `.glass-surface` (globals.css:156-157) and `.command-bar` (globals.css:1180-1181). The operator's decision surface is the only backdrop pane that doesn't pull nebula color into the pane, and on WebKit it renders as flat translucent plastic with no blur whatsoever.
items.fix: Replace globals.css:1648 with the two canonical lines: `backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08);` and `-webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08);`.

items.name: Approval panel misses the established glass shadow recipe (no specular top edge, no inner bottom shade, no depth shadow)
items.evidence: globals.css:1647 — `.approval-panel` box-shadow is only the amber halo `0 0 26px rgba(255, 180, 84, 0.14)`. Both sibling glass panes carry the full material stack: `inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 -14px 28px -18px rgba(0,0,0,0.5), 0 24px 70px rgba(0,0,0,0.45)` (glass-surface globals.css:152-155; command-bar globals.css:1176-1179). Without the inset specular the panel violates the file's own one-light-from-above law (see comments at globals.css:689 and 1039-1040), and without the 24px/70px drop it floats with no weight above the command bar.
items.fix: globals.css:1647 → `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1), inset 0 -14px 28px -18px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), 0 24px 70px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.45), 0 0 26px rgba(255, 180, 84, 0.14);` — full canon stack, amber halo appended last so the YELLOW-zone signal is untouched.

items.name: Approval panel corner radius drifts off the panel tier (10px vs the 14px command bar it sits directly above)
items.evidence: globals.css:1646 — `.approval-panel { border-radius: 10px; }`. The panel-tier radii are 16px for the side consoles (`.glass-surface`, globals.css:151; `.console-surge`, globals.css:443) and 14px for the command bar (globals.css:1174). 10px is the BUTTON-tier radius in this file (execute-button globals.css:1271, command-chip globals.css:1210), so the approval pane reads as a control, not a pane, while stacked flush above a 14px pane.
items.fix: globals.css:1646 → `border-radius: 14px;` — matches the command bar it visually docks onto.

items.name: Grain gap: the approval panel is the only glass pane without the photographic grain layer

Both consoles and the command bar render `<i className="glass-grain" aria-hidden />` (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1012, 1072, 1141), but ApprovalPanel.tsx:72 renders `<section className="approval-panel">` with no glass-grain child — and its large flat dark fill rgba(14,11,6,0.78) over a 42vh area is exactly where banding shows. sound.summary: Sound-design review of src/lib/soundEngine.ts (cross-checked against cognitionBus.ts and aiosAdapter.ts publishers). Two must-fix items: VERIFICATION RED currently plays the same bright success tick as acquired knowledge (soundEngine.ts:80), and stopSound() hard-stops the ambient oscillators at up to ~0.058 gain and closes the context mid-flight, producing an audible pop (lines 138-142, 153). Gain staging: batched trail polls fire N phase-coherent 1318.5 Hz ticks at the same instant (~+20 dB over one tick for 10 trails) — fix by 70 ms staggering. Masking: the 98 Hz reject thud sits inside the 55/110.4 Hz hum bed at half its level. Palette gaps: TRAIL WEAKENED, AI-OS LINK LOST/ESTABLISHED, and AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN are all silent today. Lifecycle: double-start is guarded, but close/reopen racing can exhaust browser AudioContext slots, and suspended-context scheduling causes a burst-click on resume. All fixes are additive micro-refinements with exact Hz/gain/seconds values; no existing sound is removed or redesigned.

items.name: VERIFICATION RED plays the success tick
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:72-81 — the 'knowledge-acquired' case only special-cases labels starting 'SKILL MASTERED'; everything else falls to line 80's bright E6 tick blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028). But src/lib/aiosAdapter.ts:104-110 publishes 'knowledge-acquired' with label 'VERIFICATION RED' for [VERIFY FAIL] — a failed verification is sonically identical to acquired knowledge. The ear is told the opposite of the truth.
items.fix: In onCognition, before the generic tick at soundEngine.ts:80, add: if ((event.label ?? '') === 'VERIFICATION RED') { blip(622.3, 0.22, 0.030, 'triangle'); blip(587.3, 0.34, 0.028, 'triangle', 0.12); return; } — a falling D#5→D5 semitone in the tick's own register: the tick's shadow, not its twin. Keeps the existing GREEN/success tick untouched.

items.name: stopSound() pops: oscillators hard-stopped at audible gain, context closed mid-flight
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:138-142 — ambientStop calls oscA.stop()/oscB.stop()/lfo.stop() with zero release while amb.gain sits at 0.022-0.058 (0.04 base ± 0.018 LFO, lines 126-130; two full-scale oscs → bed amplitude up to ~0.116 pre-master). Truncating the waveform at arbitrary phase = audible pop. Then soundEngine.ts:153 calls ctx.close() immediately, also chopping any in-flight blip/whoosh tail (sus chord is 1.6 s long).
items.fix: Release-then-stop. In ambientStop (capture ctx/master locally): const t = c.currentTime; m.gain.setTargetAtTime(0, t, 0.03); oscA.stop(t + 0.2); oscB.stop(t + 0.2); lfo.stop(t + 0.2);. In stopSound: const c = ctx; ctx = null; master = null; setTimeout(() => { void c.close(); }, 250); — nulls stay synchronous so isSoundOn() flips instantly; the device is still fully released, just 250 ms later and silently.

items.name: Coherent tick pile-up on batch trail polls (gain staging)
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:80 fires one blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028) per 'knowledge-acquired', scheduled at ctx.currentTime with identical phase. src/lib/aiosAdapter.ts:465-477 publishes one such event per reinforced trail inside a single synchronous poll loop — 10 trails reinforced in one poll = 10 phase-coherent 1318.5 Hz oscillators summing to 0.28 pre-master (~+20 dB over a single tick), a piercing spike that breaks the stated whisper discipline (header lines 14-15).
items.fix: Stagger instead of stack: module-level let lastTickT = 0; let tickN = 0; in the tick branch: const now = ctx.currentTime; if (now - lastTickT > 0.07) { tickN = 0; lastTickT = now; } blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028, 'sine', tickN * 0.07); tickN = Math.min(tickN + 1, 8); — every event still sounds (a 70 ms rain of ticks, no motion removed), peaks never sum. Optional belt-and-braces: a DynamicsCompressorNode (threshold -28, knee 12, ratio 8, attack 0.003, release 0.2) inserted between master and destination at startSound.

items.name: Reject thud is frequency-masked by the ambient hum
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:95 — the reject cue blip(98, 0.5, 0.05, 'sine') sits dead inside the ambient bed's band (55 Hz triangle + 110.4 Hz sine under a 240 Hz lowpass, lines 116-134) at roughly HALF the bed's level (thud 0.05 vs bed up to ~0.116). The operator's most consequential 'no' can be inaudible while sound is on — which is exactly when it must read.
items.fix: Keep the 98 Hz 'low settle' character but lift it clear of the bed: change line 95 to blip(98, 0.5, 0.08, 'sine'); blip(196, 0.28, 0.020, 'sine'); — peak 0.05→0.08 plus a quiet 196 Hz octave partial whose onset sits above the bed's spectral mass. Two values changed, one line added; same sound, now audible.

items.name: Bus palette gaps: TRAIL WEAKENED, LINK LOST/ESTABLISHED, AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN are silent
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:98-99 default-returns for 'agent-dispatch' and 'synthesis', yet the adapter publishes its darkest signals there: TRAIL WEAKENED (aiosAdapter.ts:502-508), AI-OS LINK LOST (567-573), AI-OS LINK ESTABLISHED (444-450), and AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN (525-531) — the tamper alarm, the highest-stakes event in the system, makes no sound at all. The header's 'what you hear is what actually happened' (lines 4-5) is currently only true for the happy path.
items.fix: Add label-gated cases (additive only, nothing existing changes): case 'agent-dispatch': if label === 'TRAIL WEAKENED' → blip(392, 0.14, 0.018, 'triangle') (dull G4, quieter and 1.7 octaves below the knowledge tick). case 'synthesis': 'AI-OS LINK LOST' → blip(440, 0.30, 0.030) + blip(293.7, 0.50, 0.030, 'sine', 0.15) (falling A4→D4); 'AI-OS LINK ESTABLISHED' → its mirror blip(293.7, 0.30, 0.028) + blip(440, 0.50, 0.028, 'sine', 0.15); 'AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN' → blip(233.1, 1.4, 0.045, 'triangle') + blip(246.9, 1.4, 0.045, 'triangle') — an A#3/B3 semitone beating at ~13.8 Hz, the only other deliberately uneasy long tone besides the sus chord.

items.name: Close/reopen race can exhaust the browser's AudioContext slots
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:153-156 fire-and-forget void ctx.close() then null the reference; soundEngine.ts:105 only guards on ctx being truthy. Rapid SOUND off→on toggling constructs new AudioContexts while prior ones are still closing — Chromium caps concurrent contexts, and new AudioContext() can throw, leaving the voice dead until page reload (double START itself is correctly guarded by line 105).
items.fix: Module-level let closing: Promise<void> | null = null;. In stopSound: closing = c.close().catch(() => undefined).then(() => { closing = null; });. In startSound, first line after the ctx guard: if (closing) { void closing.then(() => startSound()); return; } — the reopen simply waits ~one tick for the old device to release.

items.name: Suspended-context blips pile up and detonate as one burst on resume
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:31 (and 46) schedule every event at ctx.currentTime, which FREEZES while the context is suspended/interrupted (tab audio interruption, OS audio focus loss). Cognition events keep arriving via the bus, each blip stacks at the same frozen timestamp, and on resume they all fire simultaneously — a phase-coherent burst-click identical in mechanism to the pile-up finding.
items.fix: One guard line at the top of both blip() and whoosh(): if (!ctx || !master || ctx.state !== 'running') return; (replacing the existing null-only guard at soundEngine.ts:30 and 45).

items.name: Documented ambient detune beat does not actually exist
items.evidence: src/lib/soundEngine.ts:117-121 — oscA is a 55 Hz TRIANGLE, which contains only odd harmonics (55, 165, 275 Hz...); it has no energy at 110 Hz. So oscB at 110.4 Hz ('slight detune against A's octave', line 121) has no partner to beat against and the intended ~0.4 Hz breath-shimmer never materializes — the comment describes a sound the code does not make.
items.fix: One number: oscB.frequency.value = 110.4 → 165.6. It now beats at 0.6 Hz against the triangle's real 3rd harmonic (165 Hz), still comfortably under the 240 Hz lowpass — the documented shimmer appears, the dark character is unchanged.

sound.summary: Sound-design review of GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/lib/soundEngine.ts against the live cognition bus (cognitionBus.ts, aiosAdapter.ts). Two must-fix items: (1) stopSound stops the 55/110.4 Hz ambient oscillators at full gain and closes the AudioContext instantly — a guaranteed pop; fix is a 40 ms time-constant master fade, oscillator stops at t+0.25 s, and a 300 ms-deferred ctx.close(). (2) VERIFICATION RED arrives as 'knowledge-acquired' and plays the same bright 1318.5 Hz success tick as GREEN — a failure that sounds like success; fix is a label branch to a quiet 196/207.65 Hz semitone clash. Should-fixes: coherent N×0.028 stacking when one trail poll fires many identical-phase ticks (45 ms stagger + a -18 dB/12:1 safety compressor on master); the 98 Hz reject thud masked by (and beating at 12.4 Hz against) the hum's 110.4 Hz partner (move to 82.4 Hz, peak 0.075, brief ambient duck); and two honest-but-silent bus events — TRAIL WEAKENED (agent-dispatch) and AI-OS LINK LOST / AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN (synthesis) — given falling E5→D5, falling-fifth, and 220/311.1 tritone gestures inside the existing whisper budget. One could-fix: ctx.onstatechange auto-resume so OS audio interruptions don't leave isSoundOn() reporting true while mute. All proposals are micro-refinements of the existing blip/whoosh vocabulary; canon levels, motion, and the master 0.5 stage are untouched.

items.name: stopSound hard-cuts the ambient hum and closes the context with zero release — audible pop
items.evidence: C:/Users/kumar/ai-editor/GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/lib/soundEngine.ts:138-142 (`ambientStop = () => { oscA.stop(); oscB.stop(); lfo.stop(); }`) and :153-156 (`void ctx.close()` immediately after). The amb gain is 0.04 ± 0.018 LFO (lines 126, 130), so the 55/110.4 Hz hum is cut mid-waveform at up to 0.058 × master 0.5 ≈ -25 dBFS — a guaranteed click; ctx.close() also truncates any blip still decaying.
items.fix: In stopSound, before closing: `const c = ctx; const t = c.currentTime; master!.gain.setTargetAtTime(0, t, 0.04); oscA.stop(t + 0.25); oscB.stop(t + 0.25); lfo.stop(t + 0.25);` (move osc handles into ambientStop's closure with the scheduled stop), then defer the close: `window.setTimeout(() => { void c.close(); }, 300);` while nulling `ctx`/`master` immediately so isSoundOn() flips at once. 40 ms time-constant ≈ full fade in ~200 ms — release, not redesign.

items.name: VERIFICATION RED plays the identical bright success tick as VERIFICATION GREEN
items.evidence: C:/Users/kumar/ai-editor/GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/lib/soundEngine.ts:80 — every non-mastery 'knowledge-acquired' falls through to `blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028)`. aiosAdapter.ts:103-110 publishes both 'VERIFICATION GREEN' and 'VERIFICATION RED' as type 'knowledge-acquired' at intensity 1. The visuals already distinguish them (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1067 tests for GREEN only); the ear hears a failed verifier verdict as a cheery acquisition ping — the sound lies about what happened.
items.fix: Inside the 'knowledge-acquired' case, before the default tick: `if ((event.label ?? '').startsWith('VERIFICATION RED')) { blip(196, 0.3, 0.04, 'triangle'); blip(207.65, 0.3, 0.032, 'triangle'); return; }` — a quiet G3/G#3 semitone clash, 300 ms, unmistakably 'wrong' while staying whisper-level (0.04 + 0.032 summed × 0.5 master ≈ -29 dBFS).

items.name: Trail-poll bursts stack identical-phase ticks coherently — N×0.028 peak with no limiter
items.evidence: C:/Users/kumar/ai-editor/GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/lib/soundEngine.ts:80 — the tick is always 1318.5 Hz sine scheduled at `ctx.currentTime + 0` (blip delayS default 0, line 29-31). aiosAdapter.ts:465-511 publishes one 'knowledge-acquired' per reinforced trail inside a single synchronous for-loop, so K reinforced trails in one poll produce K oscillators at the same frequency, same start time, same phase: amplitudes add perfectly (K=15 → 0.42 pre-master). SSE tool_result frames (aiosAdapter.ts:113) can pile on in the same JS tick.
items.fix: Two micro-changes: (1) stagger same-frame ticks — module var `let tickQueue = 0;` reset via `queueMicrotask(() => { tickQueue = 0; })`, and call `blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028, 'sine', 0.045 * tickQueue++)` so bursts become a 45 ms arpeggiated ripple instead of one summed spike; (2) insert a safety limiter once in startSound: `const comp = ctx.createDynamicsCompressor(); comp.threshold.value = -18; comp.knee.value = 6; comp.ratio.value = 12; comp.attack.value = 0.002; comp.release.value = 0.25; master.connect(comp).connect(ctx.destination);` (replacing line 112's direct connect). Transparent at canon levels, only acts on pile-ups.
