items.name: No recovery from OS-level AudioContext suspension — isSoundOn() lies after an interruption
items.evidence: C:/Users/kumar/ai-editor/GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/lib/soundEngine.ts:108-109 — `ctx = new Ctor(); void ctx.resume();` is the only resume ever issued. On phone-call/Bluetooth-switch/tab-audio interruptions (Safari 'interrupted', Chrome 'suspended'), the context suspends and never resumes; `isSoundOn()` (line 25-27) still returns true because ctx is non-null, so the SOUND toggle shows on while the organism is mute.
items.fix: After line 109 add: `ctx.onstatechange = () => { if (ctx && ctx.state === 'suspended') void ctx.resume(); };` and clear it in stopSound (`c.onstatechange = null;` before the deferred close from finding 1) so the close-path's natural suspension doesn't fight the fade-out.

space-shaders.summary: Seven micro-refinements, no redesigns: the two must-fixes both attack skill-star findability — MemoryGalaxy's colors are authored in display-space inside a pipeline the horizon header itself documents as linear (icy cyan washes to near-white, quarantine red to salmon), and fresh weak trails peak at alpha 0.22 right on top of the galactic band's 88%-occupancy star carpet. Should-fixes: galaxy twinkle (1.7) and quarantine pulse (3.0) sit inside the sky's 1.2-3.8 rad/s twinkle band killing motion segmentation; the glyph atlas has no mip gutter so 2px glyphs render as hard grey squares past the 0.1 discard; and the equirect seam (noise cut + fwidth blowup) is on the visible -x meridian. Could-fixes: mid-layer dust occludes the near parallax star layer (depth inversion), and zero-initialized aFlash fires a galaxy-wide flash burst at mount. Every fix preserves existing motion, amplitudes, and the canon look — exact replacement values given per line.

items.name: MemoryGalaxy colors authored in display-space inside a linear pipeline — skill-star hue washes into the sky's blue-white
items.evidence: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:82 `vec3 color = mix(vec3(0.62, 0.88, 1.0), vec3(1.0, 0.3, 0.24), stain);` — these are sRGB display values, but KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:9-12 documents that the EffectComposer applies linear->sRGB on output (the exact 'purple-wash bug' class the horizon was rewritten to fix). On screen the icy cyan becomes ~(0.81, 0.95, 1.0), nearly identical to the sky's blue-white blackbody tint vec3(0.80, 0.88, 1.0) at KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:131, and the quarantine red desaturates to salmon (~1.0, 0.58, 0.52) — both hue separators the operator relies on to FIND and triage skill-stars collapse. Color temperature drift between layers: horizon is linear-correct, galaxy is not.
items.fix: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:82 -> `vec3 color = mix(vec3(0.3424, 0.7484, 1.0), vec3(1.0, 0.0732, 0.0470), stain);` (exact sRGB->linear of the current authored values, same convention as the horizon's C_TEAL/C_AMBER comments). No look change intended — this restores the authored icy-cyan and alarm-red on screen.

items.name: Weak (newest) skill-stars sink below the galactic band's star carpet
items.evidence: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:83 `float alpha = core * (0.22 + 0.78 * vStrength) * twinkle + vFlash * core;` with the soft `core *= core;` profile (line 77-78): a fresh trail (strength~0) peaks at alpha 0.22 in a fuzzy gaussian blob. It orbits at radius 7.5-13 directly over the galactic band (KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:165-167 band passes BEHIND the brain), where the mid star layer runs at up to 88% cell occupancy and brightness up to ~1.05 (KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:235-238, `midProb = mix(0.22, 0.88, band)`). The stars the operator most needs to find — brand-new trails — are dimmer than dozens of background grains.
items.fix: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:83 raise the strength floor: `(0.22 + 0.78 * vStrength)` -> `(0.34 + 0.66 * vStrength)` (same max, +55% floor). Additionally, after line 78 `core *= core;` add `core += 0.5 * smoothstep(0.08, 0.0, d);` — a ~2px crisp nucleus so even weak skill-stars read as solid objects against the soft-vs-hard grain, preserving the existing halo exactly.

items.name: Galaxy twinkle and quarantine pulse frequencies sit inside the sky-star twinkle band — common-fate segmentation lost
items.evidence: KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:118 sky twinkle rate spans 1.2-3.8 rad/s (`rate = 1.2 + hash21(cell + 3.3) * 2.6`). MemoryGalaxy.tsx:79 galaxy twinkle is `sin(uTime * 1.7 + ...)` and line 81 quarantine pulse is `sin(uTime * 3.0 + ...)` — both land inside the 1.2-3.8 band, so skill-stars shimmer at the same tempo as the background field and the red alarm pulse reads as just another twinkling star. The frequencies fight instead of layering.
items.fix: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:79 -> `0.85 + 0.15 * sin(uTime * 0.55 + vSeed * 37.0)` (slow breathing, below the sky band) and line 81 -> `0.6 + 0.4 * sin(uTime * 4.4 + vSeed * 11.0)` (urgent pulse, above the band). Amplitudes untouched — motion preserved, only the tempo bands separated so foreground/alarm/background occupy distinct frequency ranges.

items.name: Glyph atlas has no mip gutter + 0.1 alpha discard — distant glyph stars render as hard-edged grey squares
items.evidence: CosmicBackground.tsx:48-59: 8x8 atlas cells with zero padding under `minFilter = THREE.LinearMipmapLinearFilter` (line 58). Point size clamps down to 2px (line 212), which samples mip level ~5 where each cell is ~2x2 texels of cross-cell average alpha (~0.15-0.3 from glyph coverage + the 8px shadowBlur at line 46). That average exceeds the `if (alpha < 0.1) discard;` threshold (line 245) across the entire quad, so small/far glyphs draw as visible SQUARES instead of shaped points, and the 0.1 cutoff also chops the shadow-glow ramp into a hard aliased rim on near glyphs.
items.fix: CosmicBackground.tsx: insert after line 244 (`float alpha = texColor.a;`): `alpha *= smoothstep(0.5, 0.42, length(gl_PointCoord - 0.5));` — glyph strokes + 8px blur reach at most ~0.44 of the cell radius (50px glyph in 64px cell), so the mask only kills the mip-bled quad corners, never real strokes. Then relax line 245 to `if (alpha < 0.02) discard;` so the glow ramp stays smooth.

items.name: Equirect seam meridian is in view — nebula filaments cut and fwidth-based star discs smear along a vertical line
items.evidence: KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:150-154: `uv.x = atan(dir.z, dir.x) * 0.15915494 + 0.5` wraps at direction (-x, *, 0) — the LEFT of the default view (camera sits near z≈4.5 per CosmicBackground.tsx:194 facing the origin). At that meridian (a) fbm3/valueNoise are non-periodic in uv.x, so gas filaments and dust lanes show a hard vertical cut, and (b) `pix = length(fwidth(uv))` (line 154) explodes to ~1.0 across the wrap, collapsing `distPx` (line 104-105) so every gated cell in that pixel column draws disc=1 — a faint vertical smear of star streaks.
items.fix: Two exact edits in KnowledgeHorizon.tsx: line 151 `atan(dir.z, dir.x)` -> `atan(dir.x, -dir.z)` (rotates the wrap meridian to +z, directly behind the camera, hiding the noise seam without touching the visible sky); line 154 -> `float pix = max(min(length(fwidth(uv)), length(fwidth(fract(uv + 0.5)))), 1e-7);` (seam-safe derivative — the half-offset copy is continuous wherever the original wraps, min picks the valid one).

items.name: Mid-layer dust lanes fully occlude the NEAR parallax star layer — depth inversion under pointer motion
items.evidence: KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:238-240: `stars += starLayer(uvMid...); stars += starLayer(uvNear...); stars *= starOcclusion;` — starOcclusion is the dust field sampled at uvMid (parallax 0.06, lines 157-160 and 190-193), yet it dims the uvNear motes (parallax 0.14, the closest layer) at full strength. When the pointer pans, near motes slide UNDER dust that parallax declares is behind them — contradicting the file's own 'occlusion = strongest depth cue' doctrine (line 14).
items.fix: KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:238-240 -> `vec3 stars = starLayer(uvMid + 3.7, 420.0, pix, midProb, 0.7, 0.0) * starOcclusion;` then `stars += starLayer(uvNear + 9.2, 150.0, pix, nearProb, 1.0, 1.0) * mix(0.75, 1.0, starOcclusion);` — mid carpet keeps full occlusion, near motes keep at most 25% dimming so the occlusion cue and the parallax cue agree.

items.name: All skill-stars flash at mount because aFlash buffers initialize to 0
items.evidence: MemoryGalaxy.tsx:94-102 zero-fills every attribute including aFlash; line 56 `float flash = exp(-max(uTime - aFlash, 0.0) * 1.6);` evaluates to ~1.0 for every populated slot during the first frames (uTime ≈ 0), so the whole galaxy ignites with the 2.2x size + 1.6x brightness flash burst (lines 57, 84) for ~2-3s on load — a flash semantically reserved for 'a reinforcement / recall NOW' (line 8), so it lies on first paint.
items.fix: In the useMemo after the attribute loop (after MemoryGalaxy.tsx:102) add: `(geometry.getAttribute('aFlash').array as Float32Array).fill(-10);` — flash = exp(-(uTime+10)*1.6) ≈ 0 at mount; real bus-driven flashes (line 168-170) are unaffected. (If the ignition burst is actually beloved, leave it — but then it deserves a comment declaring it deliberate.)

motion.summary: Motion-curve review of GAG demo/gag-orchestrator (globals.css + SuperbrainHUD.tsx, plus ApprovalPanel.tsx and RegionPins.tsx for the newer surfaces). The token system (--ease-out-expo/quart/--ease-in-soft, globals.css:24-26) is well honored by the core HUD, but the newest additions fell off it: approval action buttons use raw `ease` at 150ms with untransitioned hover paint, and two inline opacity transitions (agent detail, intake h3) are dead code that never fires. Choreography gaps: the console-surge overlay and the region pins are boot-gated to opacity 0 but get no .is-booted entrance (the surge glow floats panel-less for up to ~900ms at boot-end — violating the file's own stated invariant), and the ApprovalPanel mounts with no entrance at all. State-swap snaps on non-blurred elements (link dot, shield tamper glow, pin open, mode-rail dot) can take 200ms quart transitions without touching the static-paint rule for backdrop-filtered panels. One reduced-motion hole: the Execute whileTap scale is Motion-driven and no MotionConfig exists, so it animates under prefers-reduced-motion. All fixes are additive micro-refinements with exact values; no motion removed, no canon defaults touched.

items.name: Approval action buttons ride raw `ease` at 150ms, and their hover paint isn't transitioned at all
items.evidence: globals.css:1714 — `.approval-actions button { transition: opacity 0.15s ease; }` — the only declaration in the file using literal `ease`, breaking the contract at globals.css:22-23 ("every transition/animation in this file rides on these · quart: hovers (200-300ms)"). Worse, the hover states at globals.css:1728-1730 (`.approval-authorize:hover` background) and globals.css:1738-1741 (`.approval-reject:hover` border-color + color) are not in the transition property list, so hover colors snap while only the disabled-opacity fades.
items.fix: Replace globals.css:1714 with `transition: opacity 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), background 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`.

items.name: console-surge glow pops to full strength at boot-end while its console is still fading in — the exact panel-less float the boot gate was written to prevent
items.evidence: globals.css:453-455 gates the surge only during `.is-booting` (`opacity: 0`), but there is no `.is-booted` entrance: when the class flips, the surge snaps to opacity 1 instantly while the consoles enter via `hud-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo)` delayed 120ms/260ms (globals.css:1382-1388). For up to ~900ms the powerSurge glow floats with no panel under it — the comment at globals.css:451-452 explicitly forbids this artifact for the boot phase but misses the boot-exit frame.
items.fix: Add `@keyframes fade-enter { from { opacity: 0; } }` and: `.superbrain-experience.is-booted .console-surge { animation: powerSurge 6s infinite linear, fade-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo) 120ms both; }` plus `.superbrain-experience.is-booted .console-surge--reverse { animation: powerSurge 6s infinite linear reverse, fade-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo) 260ms both; }` (delays mirror each console's own hud-enter delay). Reduced-motion is already covered by the global 0.01ms/1-iteration rule at globals.css:1546-1553.

items.name: Region pins have no boot entrance — they jump from 0 to 1 while every sibling readout enters on the expo stagger
items.evidence: globals.css:515-518 — `.superbrain-experience.is-booting .region-pin { opacity: 0; }` exists, but there is no matching `.is-booted .region-pin` animation, so pins appear instantly the frame boot ends while topbar/consoles/command-bar all run `hud-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo)` (globals.css:1374-1400). Note `hud-enter` cannot be reused directly: its `from { transform: translateY(14px) }` would stomp the pin's inline `translate(-50%, -50%)` (RegionPins.tsx:103) and cause a positional jump.
items.fix: Using the opacity-only `fade-enter` keyframe from the surge fix, add `.superbrain-experience.is-booted .region-pin { animation: fade-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo) 560ms both; }` — 560ms slots the pins into the existing choreography alongside the terminal-log/footer tier (globals.css:1394-1400).

items.name: ApprovalPanel mounts with zero entrance — the amber alertdialog pops in instantly
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:975-977 conditionally renders `<ApprovalPanel>` with no mount animation, and `.approval-panel` (globals.css:1637-1650) declares none — the most attention-critical surface in the HUD is the only major pane that skips the canon expo entrance. A one-shot transform on glass is canon-compatible: the backdrop-filtered consoles already run hud-enter at boot (globals.css:1382-1388).
items.fix: Add to `.approval-panel` (globals.css:1637): `animation: hud-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo) both;` (reuses the existing keyframe at globals.css:1360-1365; from-state translateY(14px) settles to the panel's natural static transform). Global reduced-motion rule at globals.css:1546-1553 already collapses it.

items.name: Two inline opacity transitions are dead code — the declared motion never fires, text just snaps
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:553 — `<small style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.3s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>{detail}</small>` and SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1086 — `<h3 style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.6s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>`. In both cases React swaps the text children while computed opacity stays 1, so the transition has no start/end values and never plays; the agent detail and the intake heading hard-snap on every cycle despite the declared curve.
items.fix: Convert to keyed fade-in animations: `<small key={detail} className="text-fade">` and `<h3 key={headingText} className="text-fade--slow">` with CSS `.text-fade { animation: fade-enter 300ms var(--ease-out-quart); } .text-fade--slow { animation-duration: 600ms; }` (reusing the opacity-only `fade-enter` keyframe). Same durations the inline styles already intended.

items.name: Execute whileTap scale runs under prefers-reduced-motion — no MotionConfig anywhere, and the CSS belt doesn't reach JS-driven transforms
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1000-1004 — `<motion.button whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }} transition={{ duration: 0.12 }}>`. motion/react only honors the media query with `MotionConfig reducedMotion="user"`, and a grep of src finds no MotionConfig. The reduced-motion belt at globals.css:1579-1585 pins `.execute-wrap` (the magnetic wrapper) but Motion writes its scale to the button element itself, and `transition-duration: 0.01ms !important` does not affect Motion's JS-driven animation. Bonus: `duration: 0.12` rides Motion's default easing, not a declared token.
items.fix: Wrap the hud-shell tree in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` (import from 'motion/react'), and pass the quart token explicitly: `transition={{ duration: 0.12, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}`.

items.name: Link dot and shield TAMPER glow swap states with paint snaps — neither element is backdrop-filtered, so the static-swap rationale doesn't constrain them
items.evidence: globals.css:322-331 / 523-526 — `.status-dot` live↔down swaps background + box-shadow with no transition declared on the base class. globals.css:344 — `.secure-button` transitions only `border-color, color`, so the `--tamper` red glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 14px rgba(255,92,92,0.25)` at globals.css:536) pops in while its border/color ease over 200ms — a mixed-speed state change on one element. Both live in the plain topbar (no backdrop-filter), so the "static class swaps only" perf rule for blurred panels (globals.css:1589-1593) does not apply.
items.fix: Add to `.status-dot` (globals.css:322): `transition: background-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`. Extend globals.css:344 to `transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`. Truthfulness is untouched — the same computed states, eased 200ms.

items.name: Region-pin open state: border brightens with a snap and the history graph unfolds with no motion
items.evidence: globals.css:481-483 — `.region-pin--open` swaps border-color rgba(159,240,255,0.2)→0.4 with no transition on `.region-pin` (globals.css:462-479). RegionPins.tsx:111-121 conditionally renders `.region-pin-graph`, which appears instantly. The pins are explicitly NOT backdrop-filtered (comment at globals.css:457-461), so a transition here is safe by the file's own rules.
items.fix: Add to `.region-pin` (globals.css:462): `transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` and add `.region-pin-graph { animation: fade-enter 200ms var(--ease-out-quart); }` (opacity-only keyframe; the chip's inline translate is untouched).

items.name: Mode-rail active dot and number ring snap while their parent button eases
items.evidence: globals.css:678 — `.mode-button { transition: 200ms var(--ease-out-quart); }` eases the button itself, but the accent fill + glow on `.mode-dot` (globals.css:745-748) and the brightened ring/text on `.mode-num` / `.mode-copy strong` (globals.css:736-743) are child properties with no transition of their own — the is-active swap pops inside an easing container.
items.fix: Add `transition: background-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` to `.mode-dot` (globals.css:715) and `transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` to `.mode-num` (globals.css:681) and `.mode-copy strong` (globals.css:702).

items.name: Objective bar glides at 500ms while every metric number tweens at 350ms — and its own % readout doesn't tween at all
items.evidence: globals.css:791 — `.objective-bar i { transition: width 500ms var(--ease-out-quart); }` vs SuperbrainHUD.tsx:282 — `useTweenedMetric` runs 350ms cubic ease-out for every store-bound number. The paired `{objectivePct}%` (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1059) is raw state, so the number snaps instantly while its bar arrives 500ms later.
items.fix: Change globals.css:791 to `transition: width 350ms var(--ease-out-quart);` and in SuperbrainHUD.tsx render the readout through the existing hook: `const objectiveDisplay = useTweenedMetric(objectivePct);` then `{objectiveDisplay}%` at line 1059.

items.name: Five animations ride literal `ease-in-out`, contradicting the header contract that every animation rides a declared token
items.evidence: globals.css:22-23 states "every transition/animation in this file rides on these", but `breathe` uses raw ease-in-out at globals.css:331 (2.2s) and globals.css:1099 (1.6s), `wireGlowSurge` at globals.css:557 and globals.css:1196 (3s), and `title-shimmer` at globals.css:1406 (1.2s). The token set has no symmetric-loop curve, so authors keep reaching outside it.
items.fix: Codify the loop curve, zero visual change: add `--ease-loop: cubic-bezier(0.42, 0, 0.58, 1);` (the exact ease-in-out curve) next to the tokens at globals.css:24-26, and replace the five literal `ease-in-out` references with `var(--ease-loop)`.

items.name: Skip link slides on the browser default curve
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:837 — inline `transition: 'transform 0.2s'` on the skip link: no easing specified, so it rides the UA default `ease`, not a token, unlike every other 200ms micro-interaction in the HUD.
items.fix: Change SuperbrainHUD.tsx:837 to `transition: 'transform 200ms var(--ease-out-quart)'`.

motion.summary: Motion-curve review of globals.css + SuperbrainHUD.tsx (plus RegionPins.tsx/ApprovalPanel.tsx for the elements they style). The core token system is healthy — hovers uniformly 200ms quart, boot choreography 650ms expo with clean staggers, and the reduced-motion clamp plus JS-side prefersReducedMotion() checks cover almost everything. The gaps cluster on the NEWER surfaces: the approval panel (no entrance, off-token 150ms `ease` buttons) and the region pins (boot-gated to opacity:0 but given no is-booted entrance; open/unfold snaps). Three truthful-status swaps pop instead of easing (link dot color, shield tamper glow, mode-rail child accents — all on non-backdrop-filtered paint, so safe to transition). Two inline opacity transitions (agent detail, intake h3) are dead code that never fires because only text content swaps. Two holes against the file's own stated rules: the objective bar transitions `width` inside a backdrop-filtered pane (the documented layout+re-blur trap) and the Execute whileTap scale runs through motion/react inline styles, which the CSS reduced-motion clamp cannot reach. All fixes are micro-refinements with exact values riding the existing --ease-out-expo/quart tokens; no canon motion is removed or redesigned, and sky/surface defaults are untouched.

items.name: Approval action buttons ride generic `ease`, not the declared tokens
items.evidence: globals.css:1714 — `.approval-actions button { transition: opacity 0.15s ease; }`. The token contract at globals.css:22-26 says every transition rides --ease-out-expo/quart/in-soft, and every other hover/state transition in the file is 200ms var(--ease-out-quart) (e.g. :344, :841, :1023). This newer approval element is the only declaration on raw `ease`, and at an off-system 150ms.
items.fix: Change globals.css:1714 to `transition: opacity 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`

items.name: Region pins have a boot gate but no boot entrance — they pop while every sibling choreographs
items.evidence: globals.css:516-518 hides pins during boot (`.superbrain-experience.is-booting .region-pin { opacity: 0 }`), but the is-booted entrance block at globals.css:1374-1400 covers topbar/consoles/command-bar/footer/terminal and never `.region-pin` — pins snap 0→1 the instant is-booted lands.
items.fix: Add `@keyframes pin-enter { from { opacity: 0; } }` and `.superbrain-experience.is-booted .region-pin { animation: pin-enter 650ms var(--ease-out-expo) 700ms both; }` (fade-only — the chip carries an inline translate(-50%,-50%), so reuse of hud-enter's translateY(14px) would fight it; 700ms slots after the terminal-log's 560ms stagger). Also append `.superbrain-experience.is-booted .region-pin` to the reduced-motion `animation: none` list at globals.css:1556-1568, since the global clamp at :1550 zeroes duration but not the 700ms delay.

items.name: Approval panel mounts and unmounts with zero motion
items.evidence: globals.css:1637-1650 — `.approval-panel` declares no animation, and SuperbrainHUD.tsx:975-977 conditionally mounts it (`{pendingApproval ? <ApprovalPanel .../> : null}`), so the operator's single most important surface blinks into existence while boot elements ride hud-enter 650ms expo.
items.fix: Add to globals.css: `.approval-panel { animation: hud-enter 450ms var(--ease-out-expo) both; }` — hud-enter (declared at :1360-1365) is from-only opacity-0/translateY(14px) and .approval-panel has no transform of its own, so it settles to natural style; the reduced-motion duration clamp at :1546-1553 covers it (no delay needed).

items.name: Pin open state snaps: border swap and history-graph unfold are untransitioned
items.evidence: globals.css:462-479 — `.region-pin` declares no `transition`; the `.region-pin--open` border-color jump 0.2→0.4 alpha (globals.css:481-483) and the `.region-pin-graph` row mount (globals.css:485-494, conditionally rendered at RegionPins.tsx:111-121) both pop instantly on click. The chip explicitly avoids backdrop-filter (:457-461 comment), so a cheap paint transition is safe here.
items.fix: Add `transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` to `.region-pin`, plus `@keyframes pin-graph-in { from { opacity: 0; } }` and `.region-pin-graph { animation: pin-graph-in 300ms var(--ease-out-quart); }`

items.name: Link dot live↔down swap is an instant color pop
items.evidence: globals.css:322-331 (`.status-dot`, `.status-dot--live`) and :523-526 (`.status-dot--down`) — the class swap at SuperbrainHUD.tsx:862 flips background and box-shadow cyan↔amber with no transition on the base class. The topbar is not backdrop-filtered, so transitioning paint here is safe.
items.fix: Add to `.status-dot` (globals.css:322): `transition: background-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`

items.name: Shield tamper glow pops: box-shadow missing from the secure-button transition list
items.evidence: globals.css:344 transitions only `border-color` and `color`, but `.secure-button--tamper` (globals.css:534-537) adds `box-shadow: 0 0 14px rgba(255, 92, 92, 0.25)` — so HOLD eases in (color/border covered) while TAMPER's red glow snaps.
items.fix: Extend globals.css:344 to `transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`

items.name: Two inline transitions are dead code — declared opacity transitions that never fire
items.evidence: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:553 — `<small style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.3s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>{detail}</small>` and SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1086 — `<h3 style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.6s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>`. Only the text content swaps; opacity never changes value, so the declared crossfade intent never renders (and 0.6s also sits outside the quart token's documented 200-300ms band at globals.css:23).
items.fix: Make the swaps actually fade by re-keying: `<small key={detail} className="text-swap">` and `<h3 key={linkUp && telemetry ? 'live' : intakeLabel} className="text-swap text-swap--slow">`, with CSS `@keyframes text-swap-in { from { opacity: 0; } } .text-swap { animation: text-swap-in 300ms var(--ease-out-quart); } .text-swap--slow { animation-duration: 600ms; animation-timing-function: var(--ease-out-expo); }` (the 'live' key keeps per-poll telemetry updates from re-fading).

items.name: Execute whileTap scale bypasses prefers-reduced-motion

typography.summary: Typography micro-review of globals.css + SuperbrainHUD.tsx. The type system is largely disciplined (one 0.12em mono-caps token, mono+tabular on most stats), but the one proportional-font element with live digits (.panel-heading h3 telemetry counts) is exactly the one missing tabular-nums (must). Five shoulds: the caps-only `case` OpenType feature is applied globally so mixed-case terminal lines get cap-raised punctuation; topbar and objective numerals inherit 0.12em caps tracking unlike their untracked siblings; topbar label/value pairs are center- not baseline-aligned (the file already does baseline correctly elsewhere); --text-3 sits at ~1.6:1 at 10px (and region-pin captions compound opacity to ~0.36 effective alpha); the approval panel mints off-scale 10.5px/12.5px sizes and 0.18/0.22em trackings. Coulds: 'Supervised' is the lone mixed-case string in an all-caps topbar, region-pin tracking is off-token and tracks its digits, the centered hero title shifts when the proportional mode number changes, and agent name/state words miss baseline alignment. All fixes are value-level micro-refinements; no motion, layout, or canon-look changes proposed.

The ONE proportional-font element whose digits change on every telemetry poll is the one missing it, so trailing text reflows mid-sentence each update.
items.fix: Add `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;` to `.panel-heading h3` at globals.css:822-828.

spacing.summary: Spacing/alignment geometry review of globals.css + SuperbrainHUD.tsx (with ApprovalPanel.tsx and RegionPins.tsx markup verified for evidence). 10 findings, all micro-refinements of existing geometry — no redesign, no motion or quality changes. One must: the approval panel is an unpositioned in-flow child of the absolute hud-shell, so it renders at the top of the viewport under the topbar instead of 10px above the command bar as its own CSS comment specifies (fix: absolute, bottom 104px + existing 10px margin). Should-level: twin consoles padded 22px vs 20px; topbar label/number pairs center-aligned in spans but baseline-aligned in the sibling fidelity buttons (file convention elsewhere is baseline); mode-rail spine at 17px vs disc centers at 19px; terminal log left edge 4px off the command-field text column (-372 vs -376); region-pin open-state graph gets 11px above vs 5px below (wrap row-gap 7 + margin 4 double-count); command-bar right inset 9px vs the 13px left/gap and 14px vertical ring; spark endpoint dot 1.5px left of the line endpoint (vertical centering already corrected in JSX, horizontal not). Could-level: approval panel 10px top vs 12px bottom insets; terminal log flush (0px) against the command bar top where the approval panel uses 10px. Every fix is a single exact value change.

items.name: Command bar right inset 9px breaks the bar's 13/14px ring around Execute
items.evidence: globals.css:1168 `.command-bar { padding: 0 9px 0 13px; }` with `column-gap: 13px` (globals.css:1172) and the 42px Execute button in the 70px bar (globals.css:1262-1266) leaving a (70-42)/2 = 14px ring top and bottom. Left padding 13px and gap 13px hold one family; the 9px right inset is the lone outlier — Execute sits 14px from the bar's top/bottom edges but only 9px from its right frame.
items.fix: globals.css:1168 — change `padding: 0 9px 0 13px;` to `padding: 0 13px;` so right inset = left inset = column-gap = 13px.

items.name: Spark endpoint dot is centered vertically on the last sample but sits 1.5px left of it horizontally
items.evidence: globals.css:953-962 `.spark-dot { right: 4px; width: 3px; }` inside .source-spark-track with `padding: 3px 4px` (globals.css:934-942). The sparkline's last point (viewBox x=100, preserveAspectRatio none, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:500) maps to the svg content-box right edge, 4px from the track's right edge. The 3px dot occupies 4-7px from the right, so its center is at 5.5px — 1.5px left of the endpoint. The JSX does the exact -1.5px half-width correction vertically (`dotTop = 3 + (28 - lastPoint) - 1.5`, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:487) but the CSS never applies it horizontally.
items.fix: globals.css:957 — change `right: 4px;` to `right: 2.5px;` (endpoint at 4px minus half the 3px dot).

items.name: Approval panel vertical insets: 10px top vs 12px bottom
items.evidence: globals.css:1652-1657 `.approval-head { padding: 10px 14px 6px; }` opens the panel with a 10px top inset, while globals.css:1700-1705 `.approval-actions { padding: 0 14px 12px; }` closes it with 12px — an asymmetric frame on a panel whose horizontal rhythm is otherwise a strict 14px (head 14, explanation 14 at globals.css:1673, diff margin 14 at globals.css:1680, actions 14).
items.fix: globals.css:1704 — change `padding: 0 14px 12px;` to `padding: 0 14px 10px;` so top and bottom insets both read 10px.

items.name: Terminal log bottom edge sits flush (0px) on the command bar's top edge
items.evidence: globals.css:1111 `.terminal-log { bottom: 104px; }` — exactly the command bar's top edge (bottom 34px + height 70px, globals.css:1164-1167). The newest log line's descender box touches the bar frame with zero clearance, while the only other element docked above the bar (the approval panel) carries a 10px gap (globals.css:1643).
items.fix: globals.css:1111 — change `bottom: 104px;` to `bottom: 114px;`, giving the log the same 10px clearance above the bar that the approval panel uses.

verifies.finding: Approval panel corner radius drifts off the panel tier (10px vs the 14px command bar it sits directly above)
verifies.note: Evidence verified exactly: .approval-panel at globals.css:1637 has border-radius: 10px at line 1646; .glass-surface 16px (line 151), .console-surge 16px (line 443), .command-bar 14px (line 1174), .command-chip 10px (line 1210), .command-bar .execute-button 10px (line 1271). The CSS's own comment (~line 1633) confirms the panel is 'Amber-framed glass above the command bar', and it stacks with margin: 0 auto 10px above the absolutely-positioned bar (bottom: 34px). So a pane-sized surface (720px wide, 42vh) carries the file's button-tier radius while docked on a 14px pane. Fix (10px → 14px at globals.css:1646) is a single-value micro-refinement: no motion/color/layout change, panel has overflow:hidden so clipping stays clean, no other rule or component depends on the 10px value (checked .approval-* rules 1637-1738 and ApprovalPanel.tsx:72). Operator sees the identical amber panel, just radius-coherent with the bar beneath it.

verifies.finding: Approval panel mounts and unmounts with zero motion
verifies.note: Verified at src/app/globals.css and src/components/ui/SuperbrainHUD.tsx (cited line numbers all exact). .approval-panel (globals.css:1637-1650) has no animation/transform anywhere in CSS, ApprovalPanel.tsx is a plain <section> with no framer-motion, and SuperbrainHUD.tsx:975-977 conditionally mounts it — the panel really does blink in while boot elements ride hud-enter 650ms expo. The fix is canon-safe: hud-enter (:1360-1365) is from-only opacity/translateY so the panel settles to its natural margin-auto-centered style; the file's 'static paint only' rules (:28-29, :1591-1592, :1633-1635) forbid PAINT animation on backdrop-filtered glass, while compositor transform/opacity is the file's own sanctioned pattern with direct precedent — the backdrop-filtered .command-bar (:1180) already rides hud-enter-centered 650ms (:1390-1392); the universal reduced-motion clamp (:1546-1553, animation-duration 0.01ms !important) covers the new rule even though .approval-panel sits outside @layer utilities, since !important beats the normal declaration. One tuning nit, not a blocker: the motion contract comment (:22-23) documents expo entrances at 600-900ms, so 650ms would match the documented band more exactly than the proposed 450ms — either value reads as the same design, only crisper.

verifies.finding: Coherent tick pile-up on batch trail polls (gain staging)
verifies.note: Evidence verified exactly: soundEngine.ts:80 fires blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028) per 'knowledge-acquired' at ctx.currentTime (delayS default 0, line 31) — phase-coherent for same-tick calls; aiosAdapter.ts:465-477 publishes one such event per reinforced trail in a synchronous loop, and cognitionBus.ts:59-67 dispatches listeners synchronously, so 10 reinforcements = 10 coherent oscillators summing to 0.28 pre-master (~+20 dB over one tick, 0.14 at output through master 0.5), violating the whisper discipline stated verbatim at soundEngine.ts:14-15. The stagger fix is canon-safe micro-refinement: it reuses blip's existing delayS parameter (already used by the arpeggio at lines 76-77/93), leaves a single tick byte-identical, keeps every event audible as a 70 ms tick-rain, and removes/redesigns nothing. Caveats for the implementer: (1) the min(tickN+1, 8) cap re-stacks events beyond the 9th at the 0.56 s slot (residual coherence for very large bursts — acceptable but imperfect); (2) the optional DynamicsCompressorNode sits on the master bus affecting ALL canon sounds including the ambient breath — it would rarely engage at -28 dB threshold (single tick ~-37 dBFS post-master), but the stagger alone resolves the finding; keep the compressor strictly optional.

verifies.finding: Stale bloom contract: comments cite threshold 0.55 / intensity 0.65 against live values 1.0 / 2.5
verifies.note: Verified at every cited location. PostFX.tsx:156-157 does say '0.55 threshold / intensity 0.65' while constants.ts:193 holds {intensity: 2.5, luminanceThreshold: 1.0, luminanceSmoothing: 0.9} and line 192 states the opposite intent. postprocessing's luminance.frag (build/index.js ~3460) is mask=smoothstep(threshold, threshold+smoothing, l) -> smoothstep(1.0, 1.9, L); knee math in the fix checks out (1.2->12.6%~13%, 1.6->74.1%, 2.2->100%), and 'PRE-exposure' is correct since Bloom precedes the AgX ToneMapping effect that consumes exposure (PostFX.tsx:7, 10-15). NeuralAura.tsx:27 (<0.5 shells), KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:219 (80% sky <0.04), SuperbrainScene.tsx:516-519 (ladder), and CognitiveGrasp.tsx:292/446-447/450-451/505-506 all match the claim. Fix is comment-only, zero visual delta — canon-safe. Two caveats: (a) fix scope misses two more stale 0.55 comments at CognitiveGrasp.tsx:64 and KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:224-226 — update them in the same pass; (b) CognitiveGrasp.tsx:446-447 is worded historically ('with bloom threshold at 0.55 the old values bloomed'), so it needs a slight rephrase rather than the literal string swap given.

verifies.finding: Reject thud is frequency-masked by the ambient hum
verifies.note: Verified at src/lib/soundEngine.ts. Line 95 is exactly blip(98, 0.5, 0.05, 'sine') in the reject branch; lines 116-134 match the cited bed (55 Hz triangle + 110.4 Hz sine, 240 Hz lowpass, amb gain 0.04 with 0.018 LFO depth at 0.1 Hz). The ~0.116 figure is the bed's summed two-oscillator peak at LFO max (2 x 0.058), so thud 0.05 is ~half worst-case bed level; 98 Hz sits in the same critical band as the 110.4 Hz component (0.058 alone, above the 0.05 thud), so masking at LFO peak is genuine — 'inaudible' is the worst-case framing (at LFO trough the bed dips to ~0.044), but the worst case is when the no must read. Fix is micro-refinement: same 98 Hz sine, duration, and envelope; 0.05->0.08 stays within the file's whisper-quiet discipline (sus-chord already sums 0.09); the 196 Hz octave at 0.020 is quieter than the quietest existing element and a natural harmonic of the same fundamental. Nit: keep the '// standing down: a low settle' comment when editing line 95.

verifies.finding: Pin chip disclosure state not exposed (aria-expanded missing)
verifies.note: Verified: RegionPins.tsx:85 has `open` state, :101-108 the chip div has role="button"/tabIndex={0}/onClick toggle but no aria-expanded (grep confirms none in src), :111-121 open unfolds the history graph — a true disclosure. Fix `aria-expanded={open}` on the div at :104 is a zero-visual, zero-motion attribute addition; identical rendered design. Adjacent gap (keyboard onKeyDown missing) noted but out of scope.

verifies.finding: VERIFICATION RED plays the success tick
verifies.note: Verified: soundEngine.ts:72-81 routes every non-'SKILL MASTERED' knowledge-acquired label to the bright E6 success tick blip(1318.5, 0.09, 0.028), and aiosAdapter.ts:103-111 publishes 'knowledge-acquired' with label 'VERIFICATION RED' for [VERIFY FAIL] — so a failed verification is sonically identical to success. Corroborating canon evidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1065-1068 already excludes RED from the synapse storm (visuals discriminate, audio doesn't), and soundEngine.ts:4-5 states 'what you hear is what actually happened'. The fix is additive micro-refinement: leaves the GREEN tick and mastery arpeggio untouched, peaks 0.030/0.028 within the whisper-quiet discipline (0.025-0.06), triangle timbre already used by approval sounds. Minor prose nit only: D#5/D5 (622/587 Hz) is ~an octave below the E6 tick, so 'the tick's own register' is overstated; the fix values themselves are exact and self-consistent. No tests reference soundEngine; aiosAdapter test asserts only the GREEN label.

verifies.finding: Vibrance can push post-tonemap values above 1.0, re-introducing the hard clip AgX was added to remove
verifies.note: Evidence is real: src/components/canvas/PostFX.tsx:107-109 matches verbatim, GradePost runs after AgX with no clamp, vibrance=0.21 at src/lib/constants.ts:206, and the example math checks (R=1.0405 for (1.0,0.55,0.6)). But the proposed fix is rejected: it fades vibrance on LUMA, while overshoot is driven by the MAX CHANNEL. Applied to the finding's own example pixel, l=0.649 < 0.75 so smoothstep=0, full vibrance still applies, and R is still 1.0405 — the cited clip is NOT fixed. Other still-clipping cases: (1.0,0.71,0.5) -> R~1.026; (0.2,0.2,1.0) -> B~1.031 (luma guard inert for blue, weight 0.0722). The fix also desaturates the high-luma crown rose the operator tuned (constants.ts:188-189, vibrance comment), i.e., a look change without delivering the claimed 'no overshoot'. A correct micro-fix must guard on cmax (e.g., fade on max(c.r,max(c.g,c.b))) or cap the scale at (1.0-l)/(cmax-l); resubmit with that form.

verifies.finding: Approval panel misses the established glass shadow recipe (no specular top edge, no inner bottom shade, no depth shadow)
verifies.note: Evidence verified at every cited line (globals.css:1647 halo-only shadow; 152-155 and 1176-1179 full stacks; one-light comments at 689/1039-1040). But canonSafe=false: globals.css:1594-1597 shows .command-bar.is-approval-hold deliberately replacing the full canon stack with a halo-only amber shadow (0 0 22px rgba(255,180,84,0.16)) during the exact state when the approval panel is visible — the halo-only shadow is paired YELLOW-zone design language, not an oversight. The panel is also authored as a distinct warm material (warm bg rgba(14,11,6,0.78), blur-only backdrop-filter), so adding the 24px/70px drop + specular would alter the operator's amber-hold scene. Bonus defect: the fix appends the amber halo last, but first-listed shadows paint on top in CSS, so the 0.45-alpha black drop would dim the amber signal — contradicting its own 'untouched' rationale.

verifies.finding: Pin chip is not keyboard operable despite role=button
verifies.note: Confirmed at RegionPins.tsx:101-108: div has role="button" (104), tabIndex={0} (105), onClick only (107); grep finds zero onKeyDown/onKeyUp/onKeyPress in src. The only global keydown listeners (BootSequence.tsx:154 boot-skip, SuperbrainScene.tsx:1115 passive idle-stamp, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:622 once-only audio arm) do not activate the chip, so Enter/Space genuinely do nothing despite focus reaching it. The proposed onKeyDown (Enter/Space -> preventDefault + setOpen toggle) exactly mirrors the existing onClick, changes no pixels, motion, markup, or CSS — pure keyboard parity with the ARIA contract the element already declares. Canon-safe micro-refinement. Optional nit: title text at line 106 says "click for real sample history"; could add "or press Enter" but not required.

verifies.finding: highTint is not luma-neutral: soft-light adds ~+3% highlight luma after AgX, nudging the crown back toward the plateau
verifies.note: All file:line citations verified exactly: highTint [0.55,0.53,0.47] at constants.ts:204 (Rec.709 luma 0.52992), shadowTint luma 0.50091 at :203, W3C soft-light at PostFX.tsx:84-91 lifts for s>0.5 (D(b)>=b proven), and the anti-plateau intent at constants.ts:188-189 / PostFX.tsx:180-181. The asymmetric-by-accident split-tone is real. One correction: the '~+3% highlight luma' figure is overstated ~5x — exact shader simulation (split-tone + balance −0.08 + vibrance) shows the current tint lifts crown-range colors (in-luma 0.77–0.95) by only +0.12% to +0.59%, peaking at +1.7% in midtones. Direction and mechanism fully confirmed. The proposed [0.52,0.50,0.44] is verified: luma 0.49992, identical R−B spread 0.08, and exactly current−0.03 uniform so the chroma deviation vector is identical (same amber hue); simulated result is near-neutral (crown −0.04% to −0.12%, midtones −0.35% to −0.41%; max per-channel output delta 0.012 ≈ 3/255). Canon-safe: one constant in the designated tuning block ('tune HERE'), same split-tone design and hue, no motion/quality/default changes, aligns with the operator's own documented crown-gradation intent. Side effect to know: net ~1.5–2% midtone dim of the golden nebula vs today (hue unchanged) — micro-refinement, not a look change. Minor nit: the finding truncates constants.ts:205 ('clean of orange' is a chroma claim, not luma), but :188-189 independently supports the intent.

verifies.finding: Pin open state snaps: border swap and history-graph unfold are untransitioned
verifies.note: Evidence verified at all cited locations: .region-pin (globals.css:462-479) has no transition; .region-pin--open (481-483) snaps border alpha 0.2->0.4 (base at :469); .region-pin-graph (485-494) mounts untransitioned via conditional render at RegionPins.tsx:111-121; the no-backdrop-filter comment (:457-461) is verbatim. Fix is canon-safe micro-refinement: --ease-out-quart exists (globals.css:25); 'border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart)' is the exact house idiom (lines 344, 841, 1023); the from{opacity:0} keyframe matches hud-enter (1360-1365) and 300ms matches line 1293; transition is scoped to border-color so it cannot interfere with the is-booting opacity swap (516-518); the 'no animated paint' rule (1591-1592) only applies to backdrop-filtered panels, which this chip deliberately is not, and the repaint is one-shot on click, not per-frame; the global prefers-reduced-motion clamp (1546-1553, * selector) automatically covers both additions. Same design, only crisper. Minor honest scope limit: the chip's height expansion when the graph row mounts still snaps (fix fades opacity only) — accurate as proposed.

verifies.finding: MSAA double-pay: canvas antialias buffer is paid for but unused, while the composer runs with multisampling 0
verifies.note: Verified: PostFX.tsx:155 has multisampling={0} (installed lib default is 8, confirmed in dist as multisampling:_=8); WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:213 sets antialias:true; dpr capped [1,1.5] high (lines 21-25). Composer mounts unconditionally on all tiers (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1301) and TierGovernor is non-visual, so geometry always goes through the non-MSAA composer input buffer and the canvas MSAA backbuffer only receives the final fullscreen quad — double-pay claim is correct, edges alias today. Fix is a pure lift: 4x composer MSAA on high tier only (tier var exists in PostFX scope, line 147), 0 elsewhere = today's effective state; antialias:false is visually inert and frees the dead buffer. Same canon frame, crisper edges; sky/surface defaults and motion untouched. Minor note: reactive multisampling prop recreates the composer on perfTier transitions (one-frame hitch, consistent with existing per-tier effect mounting).

verifies.finding: Approval action buttons ride generic `ease`, not the declared tokens
verifies.note: Verified: globals.css:1714 reads exactly `transition: opacity 0.15s ease;` (.approval-actions button). Token contract at :22-26 declares all transitions ride the ease tokens (quart for hovers, 200-300ms). Grep of all transition declarations confirms :1714 is the sole raw-`ease` outlier — :344, :841, :1023 and all others use 200ms var(--ease-out-quart) (:1580 is `transition: none`, reduced-motion). Fix (`opacity 200ms var(--ease-out-quart)`) keeps the same animated property and motion, only normalizing duration (+50ms) and curve to the file's own declared system — micro-refinement enforcing the operator's existing contract, not a look change.

verifies.finding: stopSound() pops: oscillators hard-stopped at audible gain, context closed mid-flight
verifies.note: Evidence verified at src/lib/soundEngine.ts: lines 138-142 hard-stop oscA/oscB/lfo with no release while amb.gain is 0.022-0.058 (0.04 base line 126, ±0.018 LFO line 130); two nominal-amplitude oscs through the 240 Hz lowpass put the bed at up to ~0.116 pre-master, so phase-arbitrary truncation pops audibly. ctx.close() is at line 154 (finding cited 153, the enclosing if-guard — one line off, claim still accurate) and does chop in-flight tails (sus chord blips at lines 85-87 are 1.6 s). Fix is canon-safe: identical sound palette, only OFF transition gains a 30 ms-tau master fade, stops at +0.2 s (gain ~0.1% by then), close deferred 250 ms; ctx/master nulls stay synchronous so isSoundOn() and the HUD toggle (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:629-631) behave identically, device still fully released, rapid re-toggle safe (fresh ctx). Capturing ctx/master locally in the closure is required since stopSound nulls module vars right after calling ambientStop — the fix correctly specifies this.

verifies.finding: Approval panel breaks the resaturated-glass optics: blur-only backdrop, no -webkit prefix (Safari gets zero blur)
verifies.note: Verified: globals.css:1648 is exactly `backdrop-filter: blur(14px);` with no -webkit-backdrop-filter anywhere for .approval-panel; .glass-surface (156-157) and .command-bar (1180-1181) both carry the canon `blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08)` + -webkit twin. The panel's own comment calls it "Amber-framed glass," and the file's canon note (144-146) defines the saturate-140% color-pull as the house glass recipe, so blur-only is an omission, not a variant. Fix is additive on WebKit and subtle on Chromium (background is 78% opaque, so saturate/brightness affect only the 22% backdrop contribution) — same design, crisper. Minor nuance: "Safari gets zero blur" holds only for Safari ≤17; Safari 18+ supports unprefixed backdrop-filter. Severity slightly overstated, finding and fix still valid.

verifies.finding: Pin chip has cursor:pointer but no :hover state
verifies.note: Verified at globals.css:462-483 — .region-pin has cursor:pointer (:478) and pointer-events:auto (:477) but no :hover rule anywhere in src (grep confirmed); only .region-pin--open (:481) changes border-color. RegionPins.tsx:101-107 confirms it is a clickable role="button". Cited siblings all brighten on hover (.secure-button:347, .mode-button:723, .ghost-plus:844). The fix adds one declaration reusing the exact existing --open value rgba(159,240,255,0.4) — the chip's resting border is already that cyan at 0.2 alpha, so this brightens within the element's own established hue and previews its existing open state; the ":722 hovers brighten toward white-alpha" convention applies to the white-bordered console controls, and a white hover here would actually shift the chip's hue. Additive, no motion removed, no redesign. Minor non-blocking note: .region-pin lacks a transition, so the hover change is instant vs siblings' 200ms ease.
